answer: Frescolat ML functions as a specific agonist of mouse TRPM8, inducing concentration-dependent increases in intracellular calcium in mTRPM8-transfected HEK293 cells.
Fused piperidines as a novel class of potent and orally available transient receptor potential melastatin type 8 (TRPM8) antagonists.
(+)-menthol is metabolized by human liver microsomal CYP2A6 enzymes to form (+)-(1S,3S,4S)-trans-pmenthane-3,8-diol.
Metabolism of (+)- and (-)-menthols by CYP2A6 in human liver microsomes.
Menthyl chloride is formed as a substitution (retention) product in the fragmentation of menthyloxychlorocarbene, accounting for 59% of the return product under the described conditions.
Fragmentation of menthyl- and neomenthyloxychlorocarbenes: elimination and substitution reactions of alicyclic oxychlorocarbenes.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.
Chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone via kinetic resolution of racemic carboxylic acids: direct access to enantioenriched lactone.

Methyl 3-methyl-2H-azirine-2-carboxylate (MMAC) is an aliphatic 2H-azirine compound whose structure and photochemical behavior have been investigated using matrix-isolation IR spectroscopy and DFT calculations.
Methyl 3-methyl-2H-azirine-2-carboxylate photochemistry studied by matrix-isolation FTIR and DFT calculations.

(1R,3S)-3-aminocyclopentane carboxylic acid (γ 2) is a cyclic γ 2,4-amino acid that can be ribosomally incorporated into peptides and contributes to the design of potent SARS-CoV-2 main protease (M pro) inhibitors.
In vitro selection of macrocyclic peptide inhibitors containing cyclic γ2,4-amino acids targeting the SARS-CoV-2 main protease.
(1R,6S)-6-methoxycarbonyl-3-cyclohexene-1-carboxylic acid is a versatile chiral synthon that can be obtained by enantioselective hydrolysis of the corresponding meso diester with pig liver esterase.
Chiral synthon obtained with pig liver esterase: introduction of chiral centers into cyclohexene skeleton.

Pyroglutamyl leucine is a hepatoprotective peptide identified in wheat gluten hydrolysate that, when administered at 20 mg/kg body weight, reduces serum aspartate and alanine aminotransferases to approximately 30% and 20% of vehicle-treated levels, respectively, in rats with D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis.
Identification of a hepatoprotective peptide in wheat gluten hydrolysate against D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis in rats.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be obtained in optically pure form with a yield greater than 99% and enantiomeric excess (ee) of 96% (R) via chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger asymmetric oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone using Candida antarctica lipase B and H2O2.
Chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone via kinetic resolution of racemic carboxylic acids: direct access to enantioenriched lactone.
(1R,3S)-3-aminocyclopentane carboxylic acid is a cyclic gamma2,4-amino acid that can be ribosomally incorporated into peptides to generate potent inhibitors of the SARS-CoV-2 main protease (Mpro).
In vitro selection of macrocyclic peptide inhibitors containing cyclic γ2,4-amino acids targeting the SARS-CoV-2 main protease.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.

1-deoxypaclitaxel derivatives can be synthesized from baccatin VI by selective deacylation and C-13 side chain attachment, and some analogues exhibit tubulin polymerization and cytotoxicity activities comparable to paclitaxel.
Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of 1-Deoxypaclitaxel Analogues.
Tomenphantin B is a cytotoxic germacranolide isolated from Elephantopus tomentosus L. and its structure and absolute stereochemistry have been determined.
Antitumor agents. 190. Absolute stereochemistry of the cytotoxic germacranolides, tomenphantins A and B, from Elephantopus tomentosus.

3-aminomethyl cyclohexane carboxylic acid, when incorporated as the P2 amino acid in peptidomimetic BACE-1 inhibitors, enables a novel binding mode with the S3 pocket of BACE-1, forming a hydrogen bond interaction pattern distinct from previous designs.

Ammonium acetate-acetic acid acts as a catalyst for the condensation of ketones with ethyl cyanoacetate to produce ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetate under ultrasound irradiation.

Disodium 1-[2-(carboxylato)pyrrolidin-1-yl]diazen-1-ium-1,2-diolate (PROLI/NO) is a low molecular weight nitric oxide donor that, when incorporated into electrospun polymer fibers, enables nitric oxide release upon immersion in aqueous solutions at physiological pH (7.4, 37°C).

Identification of a hepatoprotective peptide in wheat gluten hydrolysate against D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis in rats.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.
Both (2S,3R)- and (2R,3S)-hydroxycitric acid enantiomers produced by Bacillus megaterium G45C and Streptomyces sp. U121 are optically pure.
Absolute configuration of hydroxycitric acid produced by microorganisms.

spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one can be synthesized via a formal 1,6-conjugated addition-mediated [2+1] annulation using p-quinone methides and sulfur ylides, resulting in a highly diastereoselective and scalable process with good functional group tolerance and no need for metals or bases.
1,6-Conjugated Addition-Mediated [2+1] Annulation: Approach to Spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one.

Samarium(III) triflate (Sm(OTf)3) is used to activate benzylated glycosyl 2-pyridyl sulfones, enabling them to react with simple and carbohydrate alcohols to yield β-mixtures of glycosides in moderate to excellent yield.
A glycosylation protocol based on activation of glycosyl 2-pyridyl sulfones with samarium triflate.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.
1-(2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl-1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines as potent phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) inhibitors.

Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

Tetra(4-fluorophenyl) phosphonium (F4-TPP+) binds to EmrE and, in phospholipid bilayers, its structure revealed that it lies closer to the proton-binding residue E14 in one subunit than the other subunit in the dimer, explaining the asymmetric protonation observed in NMR studies.

Ammonium acetate-acetic acid acts as a catalyst for the condensation of ketones with ethyl cyanoacetate to produce ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetate under ultrasound irradiation.
Synthesis of ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetates under ultrasound irradiation.

Pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl isothiocyanate reacts with diamines to form bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas, with yields improved by ultrasound irradiation and reaction times reduced from 7.5 h to 0.75 h compared to conventional methods.
Ultrasound promoted synthesis of bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas.

spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one can be synthesized via a formal 1,6-conjugated addition-mediated [2+1] annulation using p-quinone methides and sulfur ylides, resulting in a highly diastereoselective and scalable process with good functional group tolerance and no need for metals or bases.
1,6-Conjugated Addition-Mediated [2+1] Annulation: Approach to Spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one.

[2H8]L-phenylalanine can be incorporated into 2-phenylethyl b-D-glucopyranoside and 2-phenylethanol in Rosa damascena flowers when fed throughout maturation, with the labeling pattern indicating the involvement of phenylpyruvic acid as a major biosynthetic route.

Biogenesis of 2-phenylethanol in rose flowers: incorporation of [2H8]L-phenylalanine into 2-phenylethanol and its beta-D-glucopyranoside during the flower opening of Rosa 'Hoh-Jun' and Rosa damascena Mill.
The tricyclic core of basiliolide B can be efficiently synthesized via base-catalyzed Diels-Alder reactions of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones, achieving good yields and exclusive endo selectivity.
Base-catalyzed Diels-Alder reactions of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones: a mild approach to basiliolide B.

7-[(2-aminoethyl)amino]-1-cyclopropyl-6-fluoro-1, 4-dihydro-4-oxo-3-quinoline carboxylic acid is a photochemically formed intermediate in ciprofloxacin degradation, identified by its structure elucidated from UV, mass, and NMR spectra.
Isolation and structure elucidation of an intermediate in the photodegradation of ciprofloxacin.

A liquid membrane microelectrode using a mixture of benzyldimethylhexadecylammonium cholate and decanol with hexadecyltributylammonium taurocholate/5% hexachlorobenzene + 0.5% bromoacetanilide in o-dichlorobenzene provides a quicker response time and lower drift for bile acid detection.
Two-barrel bile-acids-sensitive microelectrodes based on liquid ion exchanger.

Pyroglutamyl leucine is a hepatoprotective peptide identified in wheat gluten hydrolysate that, when administered at 20 mg/kg body weight, reduces serum aspartate and alanine aminotransferases to approximately 30% and 20% of vehicle-treated levels, respectively, in rats with D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis.
Identification of a hepatoprotective peptide in wheat gluten hydrolysate against D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis in rats.

Preincubation of rat hippocampal slices with 0.05–0.5 µM pyroglutamylasparagine amide improves long-term potentiation of focal responses in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synaptic system by facilitating LTP development and increasing its amplitude and duration.
Effect of pyroglutamylasparagine amide on plastic characteristics of synaptic transmission in the hippocampus.

Microporous hexagonal niobium phosphate synthesized with C6–C10 hydrocarbon surfactants by supramolecular templating exhibits strong hydrophilic character and high selectivity (95.3%) for catechol formation from phenol in the presence of methanol and aqueous H2O2.
Microporous niobium phosphates and catalytic properties prepared by a supramolecular templating mechanism.

Adenylyl(2'-5')adenylyl(2'-5')adenosine is a natural 2',5'-oligoadenylate that is less enzymatically stable than synthetic analogs with 3'-terminal acyclic nucleoside residues when treated with NIH 3T3 cell lysates, mouse liver homogenates, or snake venom phosphodiesterase.
A route to 2',5'-oligoadenylates with increased stability towards phosphodiesterases.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.
Chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone via kinetic resolution of racemic carboxylic acids: direct access to enantioenriched lactone.

A liquid membrane microelectrode using a mixture of benzyldimethylhexadecylammonium cholate and decanol with hexadecyltributylammonium taurocholate/5% hexachlorobenzene + 0.5% bromoacetanilide in o-dichlorobenzene provides a quicker response time and lower drift for bile acid detection.
Miniaturization of a liquid membrane sensor for the determination of bile acids.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

m-phenylenediacetic acid reacts with tetranuclear copper(II) complexes to yield both discrete tetracopper cationic complexes and cluster polymers, such as [Cu4{mu-(alpha-D-Glc-1P)}2(mu-m-C6H4(CH2COO)2)(bpy)4](NO3)2 and {[Cu4{mu-(alpha-D-Glc-1P)}2(mu-m-C6H4(CH2COO)2)(bpy)4](NO3)2}n.
Tetranuclear copper(II) complexes bridged by alpha-D-glucose-1-phosphate and incorporation of sugar acids through the Cu4 core structural changes.

A nonintoxicating oral dose of 1,3-Butanediol at 4 grams per kilogram administered after ethanol elimination is effective in suppressing the tremulous and convulsive components of ethanol withdrawal syndrome in rats for 1 to 5 hours.
Suppression by 1,3-butanediol of the ethanol withdrawal syndrome in rats.

In aqueous phase, 2,3-dihydroxy-2-methylpropanal undergoes radical-driven oxidation with rate constants of k(OH + DHMP) = (1.2 ± 0.1) × 10^9 L mol^-1 s^-1, k(NO3 + DHMP) = (7.9 ± 0.7) × 10^6 L mol^-1 s^-1, and k(SO4- + DHMP) = (3.3 ± 0.2) × 10^7 L mol^-1 s^-1 at 298 K.
Tropospheric Aqueous-Phase Oxidation of Isoprene-Derived Dihydroxycarbonyl Compounds.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.
Ammonium acetate-acetic acid acts as a catalyst for the condensation of ketones with ethyl cyanoacetate to produce ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetate under ultrasound irradiation.
Synthesis of ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetates under ultrasound irradiation.
N-(3-cyano-4-methyl-2-pyrrylcarbamoylmethyl)iminodiacetic acid derivatives labeled with technetium-99m have been synthesized and evaluated for hepatobiliary activity in rats.
Evaluation of 99mTc-labeled iminodiacetic acid derivatives of substituted 2-aminopyrroles as hepatobiliary imaging agents in rats II.

Adenylyl(2'-5')adenylyl(2'-5')adenosine is a natural 2',5'-oligoadenylate that is less enzymatically stable than synthetic analogs with 3'-terminal acyclic nucleoside residues when treated with NIH 3T3 cell lysates, mouse liver homogenates, or snake venom phosphodiesterase.
A route to 2',5'-oligoadenylates with increased stability towards phosphodiesterases.

Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed one-pot tandem reaction involving Ullmann-type C-N coupling and intramolecular dehydrative cyclization of substituted 2-(2-bromophenyl)-1H-imidazoles and formamide.
Copper-catalyzed tandem Ullmann type C-N coupling and dehydrative cyclization: synthesis of imidazo[1,2-c]quinazolines.
Zinc pantothenate, when administered to rabbits at 3.3 µCi zinc-65/kg body weight, exhibits pharmacokinetics that fit a two-compartment open model and is localized in the skin and fur of rabbits.
Comparative pharmacokinetics of [65Zn]zinc sulfate and [65Zn]zinc pantothenate injected intravenously in rabbits.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.
1-(2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl-1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines as potent phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) inhibitors.

In the lipids of both tumor and muscle tissues, radioactivity from cis-9-[1-14C]octadecenol incorporation occurs predominantly in the acyl moieties of diradylglycerophosphocholines, diradylglycerophosphoethanolamines, and triradylglycerols, rather than in the alkyl or alk-1-enyl moieties.
Radioactive labelling of lipids in rat neurosarcoma by intravenous injection of [1-14C]-octadecenol.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.

Acid-promoted de-tert-butylation of N-nitroso-N-tert-butylamine generates a diazonium cation in situ, which can be used for cyclo-etherification to produce substituted benzofuran-3(2H)-ones and P-stereogenic chiral dihydrobenzo[d][1,3]oxaphosphole 3-oxides.
Metal-Free Cycloetherification by in Situ Generated P-Stereogenic α-Diazanium Intermediates: A Convergent Synthesis of Enantiomerically Pure Dihydrobenzooxaphospholes.

EI-1941-1 is a novel interleukin-1β converting enzyme (ICE) inhibitor isolated from Farrowia sp. E-1941, exhibiting selective inhibition of human recombinant ICE activity with an IC50 of 0.086 μM.

EI-1941-1 and -2, novel interleukin-1 beta converting enzyme inhibitors Produced by Farrowia sp. E-1941. II. Taxonomy of producing strain, fermentation, isolation, physico-chemical properties, and biological properties.

Trifluoromethylphenylimidazole (TRIM) selectively inhibits capacitative calcium entry (CCE) in mouse anococcygeus smooth muscle, producing a concentration-dependent relaxation of thapsigargin-induced tone with an EC50 of 42 µM.
Selective inhibition of thapsigargin-induced contraction and capacitative calcium entry in mouse anococcygeus by trifluoromethylphenylimidazole (TRIM).

2-phenylpyrido[1,2-e]purin-4(3H)-one derivatives with piperazinyl sulfonamide substituents have been developed as potential PDE5 inhibitors for the treatment of erectile dysfunction, exhibiting improved selectivity over PDE1 and PDE6.

1-(Benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-(4-chlorophenyl)ethanol acts as a positive allosteric modulator (PAM) of the calcium-sensing receptor (CaSR) in a functional cell-based assay.

Cyclopropeniminium ions can be ligated with bioorthogonal phosphines via formal 1,2-addition to a pi-system in aqueous solution.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.
The conversion of 3H-squalene to sterols by rat liver microsomes and cytosol is inhibited by individual rat and human plasma lipoproteins at various concentrations.

The computationally derived conformer leading to the asynchronous transition structure in squalene cyclization closely resembles the analogous region of 2-azasqualene encapsulated by squalene-hopene cyclase.

4-(3,7,11,15-tetramethyl-6,10,14-hexadecatrienoyl)morpholine (E-0712) protects against liver injury induced by D-galactosamine and carbon tetrachloride in rats, as evidenced by reduced SGPT values and prothrombin time prolongation.

4-nitro-4-penten-1-ol acetate and its analogs act as inhibitors of Walker carcinosarcoma 256 cells, with inhibitory activity dependent on the length of the aliphatic side chain and substitution at the terminal positions.
Experimental tumor inhibitors. Antitumor acitivity of esters of omega-aryl-psi-nitro-psi-alken-1-ol and related compounds.

1-deoxypaclitaxel derivatives can be synthesized from baccatin VI by selective deacylation and C-13 side chain attachment, and some analogues exhibit tubulin polymerization and cytotoxicity activities comparable to paclitaxel.
Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of 1-Deoxypaclitaxel Analogues.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.
N-(3-cyano-4-methyl-2-pyrrylcarbamoylmethyl)iminodiacetic acid derivatives labeled with technetium-99m have been synthesized and evaluated for hepatobiliary activity in rats.
Evaluation of 99mTc-labeled iminodiacetic acid derivatives of substituted 2-aminopyrroles as hepatobiliary imaging agents in rats II.
Ammonium acetate-acetic acid acts as a catalyst for the condensation of ketones with ethyl cyanoacetate to produce ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetate under ultrasound irradiation.
Synthesis of ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetates under ultrasound irradiation.

2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol is a novel chiral ligand used to synthesize dinuclear platinum(II) complexes with demonstrated in vitro cytotoxicity against human HEPG-2, A549, and HCT-116 cell lines.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.

N-hydroxyindoles can be synthesized in high purity and excellent yield (>90%) via lead-promoted intramolecular reductive cyclization of o-nitrobenzyl ketones and aldehydes under transfer hydrogenation conditions.
A general synthesis of N-hydroxyindoles.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.
Chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone via kinetic resolution of racemic carboxylic acids: direct access to enantioenriched lactone.

spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one can be synthesized via a formal 1,6-conjugated addition-mediated [2+1] annulation using p-quinone methides and sulfur ylides, resulting in a highly diastereoselective and scalable process with good functional group tolerance and no need for metals or bases.
1,6-Conjugated Addition-Mediated [2+1] Annulation: Approach to Spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one.

Pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl isothiocyanate reacts with diamines to form bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas, with yields improved by ultrasound irradiation and reaction times reduced from 7.5 h to 0.75 h compared to conventional methods.
Ultrasound promoted synthesis of bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas.
Ginsenoside Rh7 suppresses the expression of ILF3-AS1, which competitively binds with miR-212, resulting in down-regulation of SMAD1 and suppression of non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) progression.
Ginsenoside Rh7 Suppresses Proliferation, Migration and Invasion of NSCLC Cells Through Targeting ILF3-AS1 Mediated miR-212/SMAD1 Axis.

Ginsenoside Rh7 can regulate the expression of ILF3-AS1, and by competitively binding with miR-212, it can enhance the miR-212-Smad1 axis, thereby significantly inhibiting the proliferation, migration, and invasion of NSCLC cells.

5-Iodo-2-pyrimidinone-2'-deoxyribose (IPdR) is converted to 5-iodo-deoxyuridine (IUdR) by aldehyde oxidase in the liver, a reaction that does not require exogenous cofactors and is not catalyzed by mixed-function oxidases, xanthine oxidase, or many other oxido-reductases.

Copper-catalyzed tandem Ullmann type C-N coupling and dehydrative cyclization: synthesis of imidazo[1,2-c]quinazolines.

12-(4-azido-2-nitrophenoxy)-stearoyl[1-14C]glucosamine is a glycolipid photoprobe capable of undergoing transbilayer flip-flop and intermembrane transfer between liposomes.
Transbilayer migration (flip-flop) of 12-(4-azido-2-nitrophenoxy)stearoyl glucosamine in large unilamellar phospholipid vesicles.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.

Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed one-pot tandem reaction involving Ullmann-type C-N coupling and intramolecular dehydrative cyclization of substituted 2-(2-bromophenyl)-1H-imidazoles and formamide.
Copper-catalyzed tandem Ullmann type C-N coupling and dehydrative cyclization: synthesis of imidazo[1,2-c]quinazolines.

Adenylyl(2'-5')adenylyl(2'-5')adenosine is a natural 2',5'-oligoadenylate that is less enzymatically stable than synthetic analogs with 3'-terminal acyclic nucleoside residues when treated with NIH 3T3 cell lysates, mouse liver homogenates, or snake venom phosphodiesterase.
A route to 2',5'-oligoadenylates with increased stability towards phosphodiesterases.

Pyroglutamyl leucine is a hepatoprotective peptide identified in wheat gluten hydrolysate that, when administered at 20 mg/kg body weight, reduces serum aspartate and alanine aminotransferases to approximately 30% and 20% of vehicle-treated levels, respectively, in rats with D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis.
Identification of a hepatoprotective peptide in wheat gluten hydrolysate against D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis in rats.
Pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia under air.
Synthesis of pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives through copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia.
N-(3-cyano-4-methyl-2-pyrrylcarbamoylmethyl)iminodiacetic acid derivatives labeled with technetium-99m have been synthesized and evaluated for hepatobiliary activity in rats.
Evaluation of 99mTc-labeled iminodiacetic acid derivatives of substituted 2-aminopyrroles as hepatobiliary imaging agents in rats II.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.

Microporous hexagonal niobium phosphate synthesized with C6–C10 hydrocarbon surfactants by supramolecular templating exhibits strong hydrophilic character and high selectivity (95.3%) for catechol formation from phenol in the presence of methanol and aqueous H2O2.
Microporous niobium phosphates and catalytic properties prepared by a supramolecular templating mechanism.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.

Preincubation of rat hippocampal slices with 0.05–0.5 µM pyroglutamylasparagine amide improves long-term potentiation of focal responses in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synaptic system by facilitating LTP development and increasing its amplitude and duration.

2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.

2′-deoxyguanosine-5′-tetraphosphate can be efficiently incorporated into DNA during polymerase reactions when modified with four different length coumarin-PEG tags at the 5′-terminal phosphate, allowing for single-molecule discrimination of the tags by nanopore ionic current blockade.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.

A liquid membrane microelectrode using a mixture of benzyldimethylhexadecylammonium cholate and decanol with hexadecyltributylammonium taurocholate/5% hexachlorobenzene + 0.5% bromoacetanilide in o-dichlorobenzene provides a quicker response time and lower drift for bile acid detection.
Two-barrel bile-acids-sensitive microelectrodes based on liquid ion exchanger.

N-(Dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol serves as a lead compound for developing diagnostic compounds for tracking mutant beta-galactosidase and aberrant GM1 gangliosides by live cell imaging.

N-hydroxyindoles can be synthesized in high purity and excellent yield (>90%) via lead-promoted intramolecular reductive cyclization of o-nitrobenzyl ketones and aldehydes under transfer hydrogenation conditions.

Trifluoromethylphenylimidazole (TRIM) selectively inhibits capacitative calcium entry (CCE) in mouse anococcygeus smooth muscle, producing a concentration-dependent relaxation of thapsigargin-induced tone with an EC50 of 42 µM.

The combination of l-arginine aspartate-adenosine monophosphate administered orally at 8 g l-arginine aspartate and 200 mg adenosine monophosphate, 1–2 hours before sexual intercourse, is effective and well tolerated for intermittent treatment of mild-to-moderate erectile dysfunction, as shown in a pilot phase II study.

Quinuclidin-4-yl thiazolylcarbamate derivatives act as long-acting muscarinic antagonists (LAMAs), with ASP9133 exhibiting selective inhibition of bronchoconstriction and a more rapid onset of action than tiotropium bromide in a rat model.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.

Structural basis for the enzymatic formation of the key strawberry flavor compound 4-hydroxy-2,5-dimethyl-3(2H)-furanone.

In the crystal, 5-(5,7-dihydroxy-4-oxo-4H-chromenyl)-2-hydroxybenzenesulfonic acid dihydrate molecules are linked by classical O–H···O and C–H···O intra- and intermolecular hydrogen bonds and aromatic pi–pi stacking interactions, with a centroid-centroid distance of 3.4523 Å between the 1,4-pyranone rings and benzene rings and 3.6337 Å between the benzene rings.
Genistein-3'-sulfonic acid dihydrate.

Cyrtominetin inhibits the hemolytic activity of α-hemolysin (α-HL) by binding to the 'Loop' region of the protein, blocking the conformational transition from monomer to oligomer and thereby inhibiting protein activity, as shown by molecular dynamics simulations and free energy calculations.
Molecular insight into the inhibition mechanism of cyrtominetin to α-hemolysin by molecular dynamics simulation.

Pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl isothiocyanate reacts with diamines to form bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas, with yields improved by ultrasound irradiation and reaction times reduced from 7.5 h to 0.75 h compared to conventional methods.
Ultrasound promoted synthesis of bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas.

N-(5-dimethylaminonaphthalene-1-sulfonyl)-3-aminobenzene boronic acid is an active-site-directed fluorescent inhibitor of lipoprotein lipase, binding with an association constant (K_a) of 1.1 × 10^6 M^-1.
N-(5-dimethylaminonaphthalene-1-sulfonyl)-3-aminobenzene boronic acid as an active-site-directed fluorescent probe of lipoprotein lipase.

4-hydroxycinnamic ethyl ester derivatives, both monomeric and dimeric, exhibit antioxidant activity against copper-catalyzed human LDL oxidation, with lower oxidation potential and IC50 values for the monomeric series: sinapate > ferulate > p-coumarate.
4-hydroxycinnamic ethyl ester derivatives and related dehydrodimers: Relationship between oxidation potential and protective effects against oxidation of low-density lipoproteins.
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives are a new class of small, nonpeptidic inhibitors of peptide deformylase (PDF).
Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives as peptide deformylase inhibitors and potential antibacterial agents.

Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed one-pot tandem reaction involving Ullmann-type C-N coupling and intramolecular dehydrative cyclization of substituted 2-(2-bromophenyl)-1H-imidazoles and formamide.
Copper-catalyzed tandem Ullmann type C-N coupling and dehydrative cyclization: synthesis of imidazo[1,2-c]quinazolines.

Microporous hexagonal niobium phosphate synthesized with C6–C10 hydrocarbon surfactants by supramolecular templating exhibits strong hydrophilic character and high selectivity (95.3%) for catechol formation from phenol in the presence of methanol and aqueous H2O2.
Microporous niobium phosphates and catalytic properties prepared by a supramolecular templating mechanism.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.

Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.

Mirabilin is a highly unsaturated macrolide lactam isolated from the aqueous extract of the lithistid demosponge Siliquariaspongia mirabilis and is responsible for the observed anti-tumor activity in HCT-116 human colon carcinoma cells.
Mirabalin, [corrected] an antitumor macrolide lactam from the marine sponge Siliquariaspongia mirabilis.

Adenylyl(2'-5')adenylyl(2'-5')adenosine is a natural 2',5'-oligoadenylate that is less enzymatically stable than synthetic analogs with 3'-terminal acyclic nucleoside residues when treated with NIH 3T3 cell lysates, mouse liver homogenates, or snake venom phosphodiesterase.
A route to 2',5'-oligoadenylates with increased stability towards phosphodiesterases.

Mirabilin is a highly unsaturated macrolide lactam isolated from the marine sponge Siliquariaspongia mirabilis, characterized by a 35-membered macrolide lactam ring, a pentadiene conjugated system, a tetrasubstituted tetrahydropyran ring, and a linear polyketide moiety attached via an amide linkage.
Mirabalin, [corrected] an antitumor macrolide lactam from the marine sponge Siliquariaspongia mirabilis.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.
Chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone via kinetic resolution of racemic carboxylic acids: direct access to enantioenriched lactone.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.
Ginsenoside Rh7 suppresses the expression of ILF3-AS1, which competitively binds with miR-212, resulting in down-regulation of SMAD1 and suppression of non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) progression.

Preincubation of rat hippocampal slices with 0.05–0.5 µM pyroglutamylasparagine amide improves long-term potentiation of focal responses in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synaptic system by facilitating LTP development and increasing its amplitude and duration.

Ginsenoside Rh7 can regulate the expression of ILF3-AS1, and by competitively binding with miR-212, it can enhance the miR-212-Smad1 axis, thereby significantly inhibiting the proliferation, migration, and invasion of NSCLC cells.

Pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia under air.
Synthesis of pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives through copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.

Benzo[a]furo[2,3-c]phenazine-11-carboxylic acid (TCH-1116) inhibits the association between p47(phox) and p22(phox), p47(phox) and PKC isoforms, PKB/Akt and p47(phox), PAK1 and p47(phox), and Akt phosphorylation (Thr308/Ser473) in fMLP-stimulated rat neutrophils.
The signaling mechanisms mediating the inhibitory effect of TCH-1116 on formyl peptide-stimulated superoxide anion generation in neutrophils.

1,2,3,4-tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridine-10-one derivatives have been synthesized and shown to exhibit multitargeting inhibitory activity against enzymes and processes involved in Alzheimer's disease, including acetylcholinesterase, butyrylcholinesterase, monoamine oxidases A and B, beta-amyloid aggregation, and reactive oxygen species production.
Scouting around 1,2,3,4-Tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridin-10-ones for Single- and Multitarget Ligands Directed towards Relevant Alzheimer's Targets.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

(Dibenzo[b,d]thiophen-4-yl)-2-morpholino-4H-chromen-4-one (NU7441) analogs have been studied for their inhibitory activity against DNA-dependent protein kinase and in vitro and in vivo anticancer activity.
Discovery of potent chromen-4-one inhibitors of the DNA-dependent protein kinase (DNA-PK) using a small-molecule library approach.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.
Chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone via kinetic resolution of racemic carboxylic acids: direct access to enantioenriched lactone.

Microporous hexagonal niobium phosphate synthesized with C6–C10 hydrocarbon surfactants by supramolecular templating exhibits strong hydrophilic character and high selectivity (95.3%) for catechol formation from phenol in the presence of methanol and aqueous H2O2.
Microporous niobium phosphates and catalytic properties prepared by a supramolecular templating mechanism.
Isocoumarin phosphorus analogues (phosphaisocoumarins) can act as reversible competitive inhibitors of pancreatic cholesterol esterase (CEase), with some compounds showing potent inhibitory activity.
Phosphaisocoumarins as a new class of potent inhibitors for pancreatic cholesterol esterase.

spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one can be synthesized via a formal 1,6-conjugated addition-mediated [2+1] annulation using p-quinone methides and sulfur ylides, resulting in a highly diastereoselective and scalable process with good functional group tolerance and no need for metals or bases.
1,6-Conjugated Addition-Mediated [2+1] Annulation: Approach to Spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one.

Adenylyl(2'-5')adenylyl(2'-5')adenosine is a natural 2',5'-oligoadenylate that is less enzymatically stable than synthetic analogs with 3'-terminal acyclic nucleoside residues when treated with NIH 3T3 cell lysates, mouse liver homogenates, or snake venom phosphodiesterase.
A route to 2',5'-oligoadenylates with increased stability towards phosphodiesterases.

N-hydroxyindoles can be synthesized in high purity and excellent yield (>90%) via lead-promoted intramolecular reductive cyclization of o-nitrobenzyl ketones and aldehydes under transfer hydrogenation conditions.
A general synthesis of N-hydroxyindoles.

Benzo[a]furo[2,3-c]phenazine-11-carboxylic acid (TCH-1116) inhibits fMLP-stimulated superoxide anion generation in rat neutrophils by blocking PKC, Akt, and PAK signaling pathways, without affecting PKC isoform translocation, phosphorylation, or kinase activity, nor affecting cellular cAMP levels.
The signaling mechanisms mediating the inhibitory effect of TCH-1116 on formyl peptide-stimulated superoxide anion generation in neutrophils.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.
Substituents at positions 5 and 7 of the 3-phenyl-4-hydroxycoumarin ring influence its inhibitory potency against HIV-1 protease.
Structure-activity relationships of some 3-substituted-4-hydroxycoumarins as HIV-1 protease inhibitors.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.
1,6-di(pyrid-2-yl)pyrene is a substrate that can be functionalized at the 2- and 2,7-positions via Ru(II)-catalyzed C-H bond activation.
The synthesis of 2- and 2,7-functionalized pyrene derivatives through Ru(II)-catalyzed C-H activation.
Benzoylazepan-2-one reacts with amines in the presence of DTBP and TBAI to produce transamidated products under aqueous conditions with good functional group tolerance.
Metal-free transamidation of benzoylpyrrolidin-2-one and amines under aqueous conditions.
Diphenylphosphinylallenes serve as substrates in Rh-catalyzed intermolecular hydroalkoxylation with phenols to produce vinyl ethers in high yields and enantioselectivities.
Rhodium-catalyzed asymmetric hydroalkoxylation and hydrosulfenylation of diphenylphosphinylallenes.

2-(1-methyl-5-nitro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)-5-ethylthio-1,3,4-thiadiazole derivatives exhibit in vitro antimycobacterial activity against M.tuberculosis H37Rv, with MIC values ranging from 1.56 to 6.25 µg/mL.
Antituberculosis agents X. Synthesis and evaluation of in vitro antituberculosis activity of 2-(5-nitro-2-furyl)- and 2-(1-methyl-5-nitro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)-1,3,4-thiadiazole derivatives.

Antituberculosis agents. V. Synthesis, evaluation of in vitro antituberculosis activity and cytotoxicity of some 2-(5-nitro-2-furyl)-1,3,4-thiadiazole derivatives.
5-nitrothiazole derivatives bearing a diethylmethylene malonate group can undergo reduction of the nitro group followed by cyclization to yield bicyclic 2-pyridones.

EI-1941-1 is a novel interleukin-1β converting enzyme (ICE) inhibitor isolated from Farrowia sp. E-1941, exhibiting selective inhibition of human recombinant ICE activity with an IC50 of 0.086 μM.

EI-1941-1 and -2, novel interleukin-1 beta converting enzyme inhibitors Produced by Farrowia sp. E-1941. II. Taxonomy of producing strain, fermentation, isolation, physico-chemical properties, and biological properties.

1-(5-azido-6-chloropyridin-3-ylmethyl)-2-nitroiminoimidaz olidine is a 5-azido analogue of imidacloprid and acts as a photoaffinity probe for nicotinic acetylcholine receptors, showing 779-fold increased affinity for rat brain and recombinant alpha4beta2 nAChRs (K(i) 4.4–60 nM, competing with [(3)H](-)-nicotine) and high potency on insect nAChRs (K(i) 1–15 nM, competing with [(3)H]imidacloprid).
5-Azidoimidacloprid and an acyclic analogue as candidate photoaffinity probes for mammalian and insect nicotinic acetylcholine receptors.

Microporous niobium phosphates and catalytic properties prepared by a supramolecular templating mechanism.

2-(3,5-dimethyl-1H-pyrazol-1-yl)ethanamine acts as a chelating ligand, coordinating to Pd(II) via two nitrogen atoms to form a six-membered ring and a distorted square-planar geometry in the complex [PdCl2(C7H13N3)]CH2Cl2.
cis-Dichlorido[2-(3,5-dimethyl-1H-pyrazol-1-yl-κN(2))ethanamine-κN]palladium(II) dichloro-methane monosolvate.

2-(2-fluoro-6-nitrobenzylsulfanyl)pyridine-4-carbothioamide acts as an elicitor in Trifolium pratense L. suspension culture, increasing the production of isoflavonoids and flavonoids.
New synthetic pyridine derivate as potential elicitor in production of isoflavonoids and flavonoids in Trifolium pratense L. suspension culture.
N-(3-cyano-4-methyl-2-pyrrylcarbamoylmethyl)iminodiacetic acid derivatives labeled with technetium-99m have been synthesized and evaluated for hepatobiliary activity in rats.
Evaluation of 99mTc-labeled iminodiacetic acid derivatives of substituted 2-aminopyrroles as hepatobiliary imaging agents in rats II.

3-(benzyloxy)-1-(5-[18F]fluoropentyl)-5-nitro-1H-indazole ([18F]-IND1) is a positron emission tomography radiotracer structurally related to the acetylcholinesterase inhibitor CP126,998, designed for noninvasive detection of acetylcholinesterase changes in Alzheimer's disease patients.
3-(Benzyloxy)-1-(5-[18F]fluoropentyl)-5-nitro-1H-indazole: a PET radiotracer to measure acetylcholinesterase in brain.

Ferrocenium boronic acid salt activates allylic alcohols to generate transient carbocations, enabling the formation of acyclic products with methyl-aryl quaternary carbon centers and high enantioselectivity (up to 90% yield, 97:3 enantiomeric ratio) under noble-metal-free conditions.
Dual Catalysis Using Boronic Acid and Chiral Amine: Acyclic Quaternary Carbons via Enantioselective Alkylation of Branched Aldehydes with Allylic Alcohols.

2,4,6-trichloroheptane, a model compound of polyvinyl chloride (PVC), can undergo coupling reactions with iron catalysts and Grignard reagents to yield triply arylated products in good yield.
Cross-coupling of non-activated chloroalkanes with aryl Grignard reagents in the presence of iron/N-heterocyclic carbene catalysts.

(3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE) inhibits the development of type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice by suppressing delayed-type hypersensitivity to type II collagen and reducing arthritis incidence and severity.
Effect of (3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE), a newly developed anti-inflammatory drug, on type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice.

A nonintoxicating oral dose of 1,3-Butanediol at 4 grams per kilogram administered after ethanol elimination is effective in suppressing the tremulous and convulsive components of ethanol withdrawal syndrome in rats for 1 to 5 hours.
Suppression by 1,3-butanediol of the ethanol withdrawal syndrome in rats.

Pyroglutamyl leucine is a hepatoprotective peptide identified in wheat gluten hydrolysate that, when administered at 20 mg/kg body weight, reduces serum aspartate and alanine aminotransferases to approximately 30% and 20% of vehicle-treated levels, respectively, in rats with D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis.
Identification of a hepatoprotective peptide in wheat gluten hydrolysate against D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis in rats.

(2R,4R)-2,4-dihydroxypentanoic acid is an ether-linked component of bacterial glycans, specifically identified in the O-polysaccharide of Providencia alcalifaciens O31, where it forms an ether linkage with d-mannose at the O-2 and O-4 positions.
Structure of the O-polysaccharide from the lipopolysaccharide of Providencia alcalifaciens O31 containing an ether of D-mannose with (2R,4R)-2,4-dihydroxypentanoic acid.

Structure of the O-polysaccharide from the lipopolysaccharide of Providencia alcalifaciens O31 containing an ether of D-mannose with (2R,4R)-2,4-dihydroxypentanoic acid.

13-hydroxy-14-methylhexadecanoic acid exists as four stereoisomers, and total syntheses of these isomers have been achieved using asymmetric alkynylation, acid-catalyzed lactonization, selective protection of primary alcohol, and Wittig reaction.
Asymmetric syntheses of four stereoisomers of 13-hydroxy-14-methylhexadecanoic acid as potential antibacterial agents.
The hexapeptide Arg-Glu-Leu-Leu-Lys-Met, when displayed on a filamentous phage, is recognized by autoantibodies from patient ITP-2 that target the GPIIb/IIIa complex.
Characterization of autoantigenic epitopes on platelet glycoprotein IIb/IIIa using random peptide libraries.
EI-1941-1 is a novel interleukin-1β converting enzyme (ICE) inhibitor isolated from Farrowia sp. E-1941, exhibiting selective inhibition of human recombinant ICE activity with an IC50 of 0.086 μM.

EI-1941-1 and -2, novel interleukin-1 beta converting enzyme inhibitors Produced by Farrowia sp. E-1941. II. Taxonomy of producing strain, fermentation, isolation, physico-chemical properties, and biological properties.

Radical cation salts of 1,2,4,5-tetrakis(isopropylthio)benzene have been synthesized and characterized by UV-Vis absorption, EPR spectroscopy, conductivity measurement, single crystal X-ray diffraction, and DFT calculation.
Single-Crystal X-ray Structures of conductive π-Stacking Dimers of Tetrakis(alkylthio)benzene Radical Cations.

In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.

4-((4-(2-chlorophenyl)thiazole-2-yl)amino)-4oxo-3-phenylbutanoic acid (2CTAP) is a functionally selective allosteric modulator of human free fatty acid receptor 2 (FFA2) that inhibits constitutive Ca2+ levels, antagonizes acetate-induced pERK1/2 phosphorylation, and prevents damage in intestinal ischemia reperfusion injury (IRI).
Characterisation of small molecule ligands 4CMTB and 2CTAP as modulators of human FFA2 receptor signalling.
(2R,4R)-2,4-dihydroxypentanoic acid can form ether linkages with D-mannose and is a newly identified component of bacterial glycans.
Structure of the O-polysaccharide from the lipopolysaccharide of Providencia alcalifaciens O31 containing an ether of D-mannose with (2R,4R)-2,4-dihydroxypentanoic acid.
2-aminobenzylstatine is a core structure that serves as the basis for designing potent, selective, and noncovalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.
A new structural class of selective and non-covalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.

(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one (TIM) protects mouse photoreceptor-derived 661W cells from lipoteichoic acid-induced toxicity by decreasing intracellular ROS and MDA levels, enhancing SOD activity and GSH levels, and increasing Nrf2 protein expression.
(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one alleviates lipoteichoic acid-induced photoreceptor cell damage.

Arginine-glutamine-hydroxymethylbutyrate (AGHMB) promotes anastomotic wound healing in immunosuppressed rats with tacrolimus, as evidenced by similar neovascularization and inflammatory/fibroblast responses to honey.
Effect of a honey and arginine-glutamine-hydroxymethylbutyrate mixture on the healing of colon anastomosis in rats immunosuppressed with tacrolimus.
Dietary 1,3-butanediol at 4% can elevate plasma beta-hydroxybutyrate and acetoacetate concentrations and increase the beta-hydroxybutyrate to acetoacetate ratio in broilers.
Effects of 1,3-butanediol and 1,2-propanediol on growth, blood metabolites, and liver glycogen broiler chickens.
Adenosine 3'-phosphonate acts as an inhibitor of the MurMN/FemABX family of tRNA-dependent ligases, which are implicated in high-level penicillin resistance in gram-positive bacteria.
Adenosine phosphonate inhibitors of lipid II: alanyl tRNA ligase MurM from Streptococcus pneumoniae.
Deoxyglucose dithiocarbamate can be radiolabeled with (99m) Tc(V)-glucoheptonate to form a hydrophilic complex (99m) TcO-DGDTC suitable for tumor imaging.

The (99m)TcN-DGDTC complex of deoxyglucose dithiocarbamate shows high tumor uptake and good retention in S180 tumor-bearing mice, with tumor/blood and tumor/muscle ratios increasing to 2.32 and 1.68 at 4 hours post-injection, respectively.

Isoxazole-3-hydroxamic acid derivatives are a new class of small, nonpeptidic inhibitors of peptide deformylase (PDF).

1,2,3-triazolylmethylene derivatives of 6-(substituted methylene)penem are potent inhibitors of bacterial beta-lactamases and were selected for further evaluation based on their stability in human kidney homogenate, binding to human serum, and activity in a mouse infection model.
6-(substituted methylene)penems, potent broad spectrum inhibitors of bacterial beta-lactamase. IV. Kidney stability, serum binding and additional biological evaluation of racemic derivatives.

1,2,3,4-tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridine-10-one derivatives have been synthesized and shown to exhibit multitargeting inhibitory activity against enzymes and processes involved in Alzheimer's disease, including acetylcholinesterase, butyrylcholinesterase, monoamine oxidases A and B, beta-amyloid aggregation, and reactive oxygen species production.
Scouting around 1,2,3,4-Tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridin-10-ones for Single- and Multitarget Ligands Directed towards Relevant Alzheimer's Targets.
3-F-PCA is an exceedingly potent anticonvulsant in rats, being more than 10 times more potent than prototype anticonvulsants used in the species.
Anticonvulsant 1-phenylcycloalkylamines: two analogues with low motor toxicity when orally administered.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.

Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed one-pot tandem reaction involving Ullmann-type C-N coupling and intramolecular dehydrative cyclization of substituted 2-(2-bromophenyl)-1H-imidazoles and formamide.
Copper-catalyzed tandem Ullmann type C-N coupling and dehydrative cyclization: synthesis of imidazo[1,2-c]quinazolines.
Pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia under air.
Synthesis of pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives through copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia.

Preincubation of rat hippocampal slices with 0.05–0.5 µM pyroglutamylasparagine amide improves long-term potentiation of focal responses in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synaptic system by facilitating LTP development and increasing its amplitude and duration.
Effect of pyroglutamylasparagine amide on plastic characteristics of synaptic transmission in the hippocampus.
Benzo[b]pyrazolo[4,3-g][1,8]naphthyridine derivatives are tacrine analogues that have been reported as potent and selective acetylcholinesterase inhibitors (AChEIs).
Novel tacrine-related drugs as potential candidates for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.

Pyridoxal 4-N-(1-benzylpiperidin-4-yl) thiosemicarbazone (PBPT) is a thiosemicarbazone derivative designed to target five Alzheimer's disease markers: low acetylcholinesterase levels, autophagy dysfunction, metal homeostasis disruptions, protein aggregation, and oxidative stress.
A novel class of thiosemicarbazones show multi-functional activity for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.

Carboxamide derivatives of 3-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyridines or 2-(diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane)-substituted pyrazines exhibit high affinity binding to the alpha4beta2 neuronal nicotinic acetylcholine receptor (nAChR) with limited agonist activity.
Structure-activity studies of diazabicyclo[3.3.0]octane-substituted pyrazines and pyridines as potent α4β2 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor ligands.

Pyridoxal 4-N-(1-benzylpiperidin-4-yl)thiosemicarbazone (PBPT) is a thiosemicarbazone compound designed to target five major Alzheimer's disease hallmarks: low acetylcholine levels, dysfunctional autophagy, metal dys-homeostasis, protein aggregation, and oxidative stress.
A novel class of thiosemicarbazones show multi-functional activity for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.
Indolyl sulfonamide derivatives are designed as 5-HT 6 receptor ligands and exhibit high affinity for the 5-HT 6 receptor, with diverse functional profiles.
Identification of 3-sulfonylindazole derivatives as potent and selective 5-HT(6) antagonists.

Benzo[d]oxazol-5-amine derivatives have been synthesized and investigated for their ability to inhibit cholinesterases (eeAChE and eqBuChE) and blood-brain barrier permeability as potential multifunctional agents against Alzheimer's disease.
Discovery of novel series of 2-substituted benzo[d]oxazol-5-amine derivatives as multi-target directed ligands for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.

16-(3-methoxyphenyl)-9,10,11,12,13,16-hexahydro-15H-benzo [5',6']chromeno [2',3':4,5]pyrimido [1,2-a]azepin-15-imine is a potent human acetylcholinesterase inhibitor with an IC50 of 0.36 ± 0.02 µM, exhibits strong antioxidant activity (3.61 µmol Trolox equivalents), and has moderate Abeta1-42 antiaggregating power (40.3%).
Synthesis and Biological Assessment of Racemic Benzochromenopyrimidinimines as Antioxidant, Cholinesterase, and Aβ1-42 Aggregation Inhibitors for Alzheimer's Disease Therapy.

3-(benzyloxy)-1-(5-[18F]fluoropentyl)-5-nitro-1H-indazole ([18F]-IND1) is a positron emission tomography radiotracer structurally related to the acetylcholinesterase inhibitor CP126,998, designed for noninvasive detection of acetylcholinesterase changes in Alzheimer's disease patients.
3-(Benzyloxy)-1-(5-[18F]fluoropentyl)-5-nitro-1H-indazole: a PET radiotracer to measure acetylcholinesterase in brain.

(E)-2-(2-(4-(dimethylamino)styryl)-6-methyl-4H-pyran-4-ylidene) malononitrile (PAD-1) is a smart dicynomethylene-4H-pyran fluorophore that rapidly enters the brain after intravenous injection and shows a turn-on fluorescence effect upon binding to aggregated beta-amyloid fibrils.
In vivo detection of cerebral amyloid fibrils with smart dicynomethylene-4H-pyran-based fluorescence probe.

5H-indeno[1,2-c]pyridazine derivatives are reversible type B monoamine oxidase inhibitors that contain a hydrophobic benzyloxy or 4,4,4-trifluorobutoxy side chain at the C(8) position of the heterocyclic moiety.
Synthesis, structural reassignment, and biological activity of type B MAO inhibitors based on the 5H-indeno[1,2-c]pyridazin-5-one core.

1,2,3,4-tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridin-10-one is a chemical scaffold used to develop compounds with multimodal anti-Alzheimer's disease (AD) profiles, including mono- and multitargeting inhibition of monoamine oxidases (MAOs) and cholinesterases (ChEs), anti-aggregating activity on amyloid beta (Ab), and neuroprotection against Ab and reactive oxygen species (ROS).
Scouting around 1,2,3,4-Tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridin-10-ones for Single- and Multitarget Ligands Directed towards Relevant Alzheimer's Targets.

Quinuclidin-4-yl thiazolylcarbamate derivatives act as long-acting muscarinic antagonists (LAMAs), with ASP9133 exhibiting selective inhibition of bronchoconstriction and a more rapid onset of action than tiotropium bromide in a rat model.
Novel quinuclidinyl heteroarylcarbamate derivatives as muscarinic receptor antagonists.
Benzo[b]pyrazolo[4,3-g][1,8]naphthyridine derivatives have been reported as tacrine analogs with anti-cholinesterase activity.
Novel tacrine-related drugs as potential candidates for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.

N-Ac-O-AAF is a carcinogen that modifies DNA, with DNA accessible to staphylococcal nuclease containing twice as many modified bases as DNA tightly bound to histones and protected from nuclease attack.
Distribution along DNA of the bound carcinogen N-acetoxy-N-2-acetylaminofluorene in chromatin modified in vitro.
N-AcO-AAF is a carcinogen that can be released from chromatin during digestion with staphylococcal nuclease, with its release kinetics differing from those of the released nucleotides.
Distribution along DNA of the bound carcinogen N-acetoxy-N-2-acetylaminofluorene in chromatin modified in vitro.
N-Ac-O-AAF is a carcinogen that induces chemical and physical alterations in the DNA double helix, both in vivo and in vitro.
Comparative carcinogenicities and reactivities of N-myristoyloxy-N-acetyl-2-aminofluorene and its 7-iodo derivative.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.
3,24-bis(3-bromophenoxy)cholane undergoes palladium-catalyzed amination with polyamines and polyoxadiamines to form macrocycles containing steroidal and polyamine moieties, yielding 38–65%.
Synthesis of nitrogen- and oxygen-containing macrocycles--derivatives of lithocholic Acid.

Riisein B is a steroidal glycoside isolated from the Brazilian telestacean octocoral Carijoa (Telesto) riisei, with the structure 3beta-O-(4'-O-acetyl-beta-D-arabinopyranosyl)-25xi-choles tan e-3beta, 5alpha,6beta,26-tetrol-26-acetate.
New cytotoxic sterol glycosides from the octocoral Carijoa (Telesto) riisei.

1-deoxypaclitaxel derivatives can be synthesized from baccatin VI by selective deacylation and C-13 side chain attachment, and some analogues exhibit tubulin polymerization and cytotoxicity activities comparable to paclitaxel.
Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of 1-Deoxypaclitaxel Analogues.

In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.

2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol is a novel chiral ligand used to synthesize dinuclear platinum(II) complexes with demonstrated in vitro cytotoxicity against human HEPG-2, A549, and HCT-116 cell lines.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.

4-nitro-4-penten-1-ol acetate and its analogs act as inhibitors of Walker carcinosarcoma 256 cells, with inhibitory activity dependent on the length of the aliphatic side chain and substitution at the terminal positions.
Experimental tumor inhibitors. Antitumor acitivity of esters of omega-aryl-psi-nitro-psi-alken-1-ol and related compounds.

Experimental tumor inhibitors. Antitumor acitivity of esters of omega-aryl-psi-nitro-psi-alken-1-ol and related compounds.
Manadosterol A is a dimeric sterol isolated from the marine sponge Lissodendryx fibrosa that exhibits inhibitory activity against the formation of the Ubc13-Uev1A complex.
Manadosterols A and B, sulfonated sterol dimers inhibiting the Ubc13-Uev1A interaction, isolated from the marine sponge Lissodendryx fibrosa.
nebrosteroid I is a newly isolated 19γ-oxygenated steroid from the Formosan soft coral Nephthea chabroli.
New 19-oxygenated and 4-methylated steroids from the Formosan soft coral Nephthea chabroli.

A liquid membrane microelectrode using a mixture of benzyldimethylhexadecylammonium cholate and decanol with hexadecyltributylammonium taurocholate/5% hexachlorobenzene + 0.5% bromoacetanilide in o-dichlorobenzene provides a quicker response time and lower drift for bile acid detection.
Two-barrel bile-acids-sensitive microelectrodes based on liquid ion exchanger.
Ginsenoside Rh7 suppresses the expression of ILF3-AS1, which competitively binds with miR-212, resulting in down-regulation of SMAD1 and suppression of non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) progression.
Ginsenoside Rh7 Suppresses Proliferation, Migration and Invasion of NSCLC Cells Through Targeting ILF3-AS1 Mediated miR-212/SMAD1 Axis.

Ginsenoside Rh7 can regulate the expression of ILF3-AS1, and by competitively binding with miR-212, it can enhance the miR-212-Smad1 axis, thereby significantly inhibiting the proliferation, migration, and invasion of NSCLC cells.
Ginsenoside Rh7 Suppresses Proliferation, Migration and Invasion of NSCLC Cells Through Targeting ILF3-AS1 Mediated miR-212/SMAD1 Axis.

A liquid membrane microelectrode using a mixture of benzyldimethylhexadecylammonium cholate and decanol with hexadecyltributylammonium taurocholate/5% hexachlorobenzene + 0.5% bromoacetanilide in o-dichlorobenzene provides a quicker response time and lower drift for bile acid detection.
Miniaturization of a liquid membrane sensor for the determination of bile acids.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

Adenylyl(2'-5')adenylyl(2'-5')adenosine is a natural 2',5'-oligoadenylate that is less enzymatically stable than synthetic analogs with 3'-terminal acyclic nucleoside residues when treated with NIH 3T3 cell lysates, mouse liver homogenates, or snake venom phosphodiesterase.
A route to 2',5'-oligoadenylates with increased stability towards phosphodiesterases.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.
1-(2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl-1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines as potent phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) inhibitors.
(24S)-2α,3α,5α,22,23-pentahydroxystigmastan-6-one is a synthetic brassinosteroid derivative that inhibits HSV-1 and arenavirus replication in cell culture.
Antiviral effect of brassinosteroids against herpes virus and arenaviruses.
N-(3-cyano-4-methyl-2-pyrrylcarbamoylmethyl)iminodiacetic acid derivatives labeled with technetium-99m have been synthesized and evaluated for hepatobiliary activity in rats.
Evaluation of 99mTc-labeled iminodiacetic acid derivatives of substituted 2-aminopyrroles as hepatobiliary imaging agents in rats II.
polygalasaponin XXVIII is a known saponin that has been isolated from the callus tissue culture of Polygala amarella seeds.
Biologically active triterpene saponins from callus tissue of Polygala amarella.
Virgaureasaponin 1 is a cytotoxic compound that can be enzymatically hydrolyzed to produce tetrasaccharide derivatives lacking the glucose moiety at C-3 of the aglycone.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.

Mirabilin is a highly unsaturated macrolide lactam isolated from the aqueous extract of the lithistid demosponge Siliquariaspongia mirabilis and is responsible for the observed anti-tumor activity in HCT-116 human colon carcinoma cells.
Mirabalin, [corrected] an antitumor macrolide lactam from the marine sponge Siliquariaspongia mirabilis.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.

Dodecyl-alpha-D-maltoside is a non-ionic detergent that stabilizes and enables crystallization of the human nuclear receptor hRARgamma ligand-binding domain by mediating a crystal contact in the coactivator binding region.
Structural role of a detergent molecule in retinoic acid nuclear receptor crystals.

Chloromaloside A is a cytotoxic steroidal glycoside isolated from Chlorophytum malayense Ridley and characterized as neohecogenin 3-O-beta-D-glucopyranosyl(1→2)-[beta-D-xylopyranosyl(1→3)]-beta-D-glucopyranosyl(1→4)-beta-D-galactopyranoside.

(3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE) inhibits the development of type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice by suppressing delayed-type hypersensitivity to type II collagen and reducing arthritis incidence and severity.

Oblonganoside K is a taraxastane type three triterpenoid saponin isolated from Ilex oblonga leaves that inhibits Tobacco Mosaic Virus (TMV) replication with 85.7% inhibition at 0.2 mg/ml and has an EC50 of 0.076 mg/ml.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.
