Trimethylhydroquinone derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated for anti-lipid peroxidation activity, inhibition of rat basophilic leukemia-1 (RBL-1) cell 5-lipoxygenase, and reduction of 48 h homologous passive cutaneous anaphylaxis (PCA) activity in rats.
Synthesis of trimethylhydroquinone derivatives as anti-allergic agents with anti-oxidative actions.
1,7-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one can be synthesized in 19% overall yield starting from 4-methoxycinnamic acid via ethyl 5-(4-methoxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-2.4-pentadienoate.
Synthesis of 1,7-bis(4-hydroxypenyl)-4-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one, an antiplatelet diarylheptanoid from Alpinia blepharocalyx K. schum.

Adenylyl(2'-5')adenylyl(2'-5')adenosine is a natural 2',5'-oligoadenylate that is less enzymatically stable than synthetic analogs with 3'-terminal acyclic nucleoside residues when treated with NIH 3T3 cell lysates, mouse liver homogenates, or snake venom phosphodiesterase.
A route to 2',5'-oligoadenylates with increased stability towards phosphodiesterases.
Pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia under air.
Synthesis of pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives through copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.
Cyclopropeniminium ions can be ligated with bioorthogonal phosphines via formal 1,2-addition to a pi-system in aqueous solution.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.

Trifluoromethylphenylimidazole (TRIM) selectively inhibits capacitative calcium entry (CCE) in mouse anococcygeus smooth muscle, producing a concentration-dependent relaxation of thapsigargin-induced tone with an EC50 of 42 µM.
Selective inhibition of thapsigargin-induced contraction and capacitative calcium entry in mouse anococcygeus by trifluoromethylphenylimidazole (TRIM).

N-hydroxyindoles can be synthesized in high purity and excellent yield (>90%) via lead-promoted intramolecular reductive cyclization of o-nitrobenzyl ketones and aldehydes under transfer hydrogenation conditions.
A general synthesis of N-hydroxyindoles.

1,2,3,4-tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridine-10-one derivatives have been synthesized and shown to exhibit multitargeting inhibitory activity against enzymes and processes involved in Alzheimer's disease, including acetylcholinesterase, butyrylcholinesterase, monoamine oxidases A and B, beta-amyloid aggregation, and reactive oxygen species production.
Scouting around 1,2,3,4-Tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridin-10-ones for Single- and Multitarget Ligands Directed towards Relevant Alzheimer's Targets.
Isocoumarin phosphorus analogues (phosphaisocoumarins) can act as reversible competitive inhibitors of pancreatic cholesterol esterase (CEase), with some compounds showing potent inhibitory activity.
Phosphaisocoumarins as a new class of potent inhibitors for pancreatic cholesterol esterase.

Bis-chelated gold(I) diphosphine complexes, such as [Au(d2pypp)2]Cl, selectively induce apoptosis in breast cancer cells at submicromolar concentrations by targeting mitochondria and inhibiting the thioredoxin/thioredoxin reductase system.
A gold(I) phosphine complex selectively induces apoptosis in breast cancer cells: implications for anticancer therapeutics targeted to mitochondria.
KF-Al2O3 acts as a catalyst in the Claisen-Schmidt condensation of acetophenone with aromatic aldehydes, yielding chalcones with 83–98% yield in alcoholic solvent under ultrasound irradiation.
Improved synthesis of chalcones under ultrasound irradiation.
N-(3-cyano-4-methyl-2-pyrrylcarbamoylmethyl)iminodiacetic acid derivatives labeled with technetium-99m have been synthesized and evaluated for hepatobiliary activity in rats.
Evaluation of 99mTc-labeled iminodiacetic acid derivatives of substituted 2-aminopyrroles as hepatobiliary imaging agents in rats II.
5,6-bis(4-methoxyphenyl)-2H-pyridazin-3-one derivatives can inhibit IL-1beta production in HL-60 cells stimulated with lipopolysaccharide (LPS).
Synthesis and bioactivities of novel 5,6-bis(4-methoxyphenyl)-2H-pyridazin-3-one derivatives: inhibitors of interleukin-1 beta (IL-1beta) production.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.
Chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone via kinetic resolution of racemic carboxylic acids: direct access to enantioenriched lactone.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.

Microporous hexagonal niobium phosphate synthesized with C6–C10 hydrocarbon surfactants by supramolecular templating exhibits strong hydrophilic character and high selectivity (95.3%) for catechol formation from phenol in the presence of methanol and aqueous H2O2.
Microporous niobium phosphates and catalytic properties prepared by a supramolecular templating mechanism.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.
N-(3-cyano-4-methyl-2-pyrrylcarbamoylmethyl)iminodiacetic acid derivatives labeled with technetium-99m have been synthesized and evaluated for hepatobiliary activity in rats.
Evaluation of 99mTc-labeled iminodiacetic acid derivatives of substituted 2-aminopyrroles as hepatobiliary imaging agents in rats II.
Ammonium acetate-acetic acid acts as a catalyst for the condensation of ketones with ethyl cyanoacetate to produce ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetate under ultrasound irradiation.
Synthesis of ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetates under ultrasound irradiation.

(3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE) inhibits the development of type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice by suppressing delayed-type hypersensitivity to type II collagen and reducing arthritis incidence and severity.
Effect of (3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE), a newly developed anti-inflammatory drug, on type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice.
1,7-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one can be synthesized in 19% overall yield starting from 4-methoxycinnamic acid via ethyl 5-(4-methoxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-2.4-pentadienoate.
Synthesis of 1,7-bis(4-hydroxypenyl)-4-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one, an antiplatelet diarylheptanoid from Alpinia blepharocalyx K. schum.
Tetrahydrofuroyl-l-phenylalanine derivatives have been studied as VLA-4 antagonists using 3-D-QSAR (CoMFA and CoMSIA) models.
3-D-QSAR CoMFA and CoMSIA studies on tetrahydrofuroyl-L-phenylalanine derivatives as VLA-4 antagonists.

The presence of succinate acetate in vivo proton MR spectroscopy indicates the presence of degenerating cysticerci in the brain, distinguishing it from anaerobic brain abscesses where acetate is present alone or at higher concentrations than succinate.
Higher succinate than acetate levels differentiate cerebral degenerating cysticerci from anaerobic abscesses on in-vivo proton MR spectroscopy.

Samarium(III) triflate (Sm(OTf)3) is used to activate benzylated glycosyl 2-pyridyl sulfones, enabling them to react with simple and carbohydrate alcohols to yield β-mixtures of glycosides in moderate to excellent yield.
A glycosylation protocol based on activation of glycosyl 2-pyridyl sulfones with samarium triflate.

Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed one-pot tandem reaction involving Ullmann-type C-N coupling and intramolecular dehydrative cyclization of substituted 2-(2-bromophenyl)-1H-imidazoles and formamide.
Copper-catalyzed tandem Ullmann type C-N coupling and dehydrative cyclization: synthesis of imidazo[1,2-c]quinazolines.

Preincubation of rat hippocampal slices with 0.05–0.5 µM pyroglutamylasparagine amide improves long-term potentiation of focal responses in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synaptic system by facilitating LTP development and increasing its amplitude and duration.
Effect of pyroglutamylasparagine amide on plastic characteristics of synaptic transmission in the hippocampus.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

2-(1-benzyl-1H-indol-3-yl)-2-oxo-N-phenylacetamide (KI-7) is a positive allosteric modulator of the A2B adenosine receptor in mesenchymal stem cells, potentiating the effects of adenosine and synthetic A2B AR agonists on osteoblast differentiation in vitro.
Osteoblast differentiation and survival: A role for A2B adenosine receptor allosteric modulators.

Benzo[a]furo[2,3-c]phenazine-11-carboxylic acid (TCH-1116) inhibits the association between p47(phox) and p22(phox), p47(phox) and PKC isoforms, PKB/Akt and p47(phox), PAK1 and p47(phox), and Akt phosphorylation (Thr308/Ser473) in fMLP-stimulated rat neutrophils.
The signaling mechanisms mediating the inhibitory effect of TCH-1116 on formyl peptide-stimulated superoxide anion generation in neutrophils.

Pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl isothiocyanate reacts with diamines to form bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas, with yields improved by ultrasound irradiation and reaction times reduced from 7.5 h to 0.75 h compared to conventional methods.
Ultrasound promoted synthesis of bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas.

2-[2'-(methylthio)ethyl]methionine (Dmt) is a C alpha-tetrasubstituted alpha-amino acid residue that, when incorporated into the tripeptide fMLP-OMe, imparts chemotactic activity toward human neutrophils, stimulating migration, superoxide anion generation, and lysozyme release.
Synthesis, conformation, and biological activity of two fMLP-OMe analogues containing the new 2-[2'-(methylthio)ethyl]methionine residue.

Replacement of the Pro residue in the cyclic hexapeptide L-363,301 with N-(4-aminobutyl) glycine (Nlys) maintains binding to recombinant human somatostatin receptors, and the length of the basic peptoid residue does not influence binding to the hsst2 receptor.
Synthesis and binding potencies of cyclic hexapeptide analogs of somatostatin incorporating acidic and basic peptoid residues.

(5S,12S)-dihydroxy-6,8,10,14-eicosatetraenoic acid inhibits leukotriene B4-induced degranulation and superoxide anion generation in human leukocytes in a dose-dependent manner without affecting cyclic AMP levels.
Relationship of cyclic-AMP levels in leukotriene B4-stimulated leukocytes to lysosomal enzyme release and the generation of superoxide anions.

Pyroglutamyl leucine is a hepatoprotective peptide identified in wheat gluten hydrolysate that, when administered at 20 mg/kg body weight, reduces serum aspartate and alanine aminotransferases to approximately 30% and 20% of vehicle-treated levels, respectively, in rats with D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis.
Identification of a hepatoprotective peptide in wheat gluten hydrolysate against D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis in rats.

In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.

3-nonaprenyl-4-hydroxybenzoate can exist as an intermediate pool in intact rat heart mitochondria and its synthetic rate in these mitochondria is similar to that of ubiquinone.
Possible existence of an intermediate pool of ubiquinone in rat heart mitochondria.
Ammonium acetate-acetic acid acts as a catalyst for the condensation of ketones with ethyl cyanoacetate to produce ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetate under ultrasound irradiation.
Synthesis of ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetates under ultrasound irradiation.
Benzoylazepan-2-one reacts with amines in the presence of DTBP and TBAI to produce transamidated products under aqueous conditions with good functional group tolerance.
Metal-free transamidation of benzoylpyrrolidin-2-one and amines under aqueous conditions.
N-(3-cyano-4-methyl-2-pyrrylcarbamoylmethyl)iminodiacetic acid derivatives labeled with technetium-99m have been synthesized and evaluated for hepatobiliary activity in rats.
Evaluation of 99mTc-labeled iminodiacetic acid derivatives of substituted 2-aminopyrroles as hepatobiliary imaging agents in rats II.

3,5-pyridinedicarboxylic acid N-oxide (H2-pydcao) serves as an organic linker in the synthesis of the three-dimensional metal-organic framework [Zn(pydcao)(DMF)], where it acts as the ligand coordinating to Zn(II) centers.
A Zn(ii) metal-organic framework with dinuclear [Zn2(N-oxide)2] secondary building units.

[2H8]L-phenylalanine can be incorporated into 2-phenylethyl b-D-glucopyranoside and 2-phenylethanol in Rosa damascena flowers when fed throughout maturation, with the labeling pattern indicating the involvement of phenylpyruvic acid as a major biosynthetic route.

Biogenesis of 2-phenylethanol in rose flowers: incorporation of [2H8]L-phenylalanine into 2-phenylethanol and its beta-D-glucopyranoside during the flower opening of Rosa 'Hoh-Jun' and Rosa damascena Mill.

The interaction of 3,3,3-trifluoro-2,2-dihydroxy-1-phenyl-1-propanone (TDPP) with chicken liver carboxylesterase involves formation of a cross-linked adduct between TDPP, the active-site serine, and the active-site histidine, as shown by 19F-NMR studies.
New catalytic roles for serine esterases: a 19F-NMR study of the interaction of 3,3,3-trifluoro-2,2-dihydroxy-1-phenyl-1-propanone with chicken liver carboxylesterase.
The presence of tyrosyl iodide in thyroid hormone analogs enhances their inhibition of 5'-iodothyronine deiodinase (5'-ITD) activity in rat liver homogenates.
Thyroid hormone analog inhibition of hepatic 5'-iodothyronine deiodinase activity.

3-chloro-1-methyl-1H-pyrazole-5-carboxylic acid (CMPA) is an effective inducer of systemic acquired resistance (SAR) in tobacco, inducing PR gene expression and broad-range disease resistance without antibacterial activity.
Pyrazolecarboxylic acid derivative induces systemic acquired resistance in tobacco.

3-(hydroxymethyl)benzophenone undergoes a highly efficient (Φ ∼ 0.6) intramolecular redox reaction in acidic (pH < 3) aqueous solution to form 3-formylbenzhydrol (95%) with trace amounts of the fully oxidized 3-formylbenzophenone (5%).
Formal intramolecular photoredox chemistry of meta-substituted benzophenones.
p-amidinophenylphosphinic acid (AMPA) forms hydrogen bonds with catalytic residues at the oxyanion hole of trypsin, resulting in improved activity compared to benzamidine.
An oxyanion-hole selective serine protease inhibitor in complex with trypsin.

N-carbobenzoxyglycine vinyl ester inhibits pregnancy in mice when administered intravaginally and also inhibits acrosin enzymatic activity as measured by N-alpha-benzoyl-L-arginine ethyl ester and azocasein hydrolysis assays.
Antifertility activity of N-protected glycine activated esters.

N-(5-dimethylaminonaphthalene-1-sulfonyl)-3-aminobenzene boronic acid is an active-site-directed fluorescent inhibitor of lipoprotein lipase, binding with an association constant (K_a) of 1.1 × 10^6 M^-1.
N-(5-dimethylaminonaphthalene-1-sulfonyl)-3-aminobenzene boronic acid as an active-site-directed fluorescent probe of lipoprotein lipase.

Perfluorotetramethylcyclohexane (Multifluor APF-144) is a perfluorocarbon compound evaluated as a vitreous replacement agent and short-term postoperative retinal tamponading agent in rabbits, showing no evidence of endophthalmitis, retinal toxicity, or significant electroretinographic changes after intravitreal injection.
Blood-ocular barrier permeability and electroretinogram after intravitreal silicone oils of varying composition.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.
1-(2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl-1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines as potent phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) inhibitors.
Introduction of trifluoromethoxyl groups into aromatic methoxyl substituted phenylethylene diamines prevents metabolic demethylation and increases sigma receptor affinity.
Trifluoromethoxyl substituted phenylethylene diamines as high affinity sigma receptor ligands with potent anti-cocaine actions.

N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.

spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one can be synthesized via a formal 1,6-conjugated addition-mediated [2+1] annulation using p-quinone methides and sulfur ylides, resulting in a highly diastereoselective and scalable process with good functional group tolerance and no need for metals or bases.
1,6-Conjugated Addition-Mediated [2+1] Annulation: Approach to Spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one.

The C-terminally fluorinated peptide desGly10-LH-RH-2,2,2-trifluoroethylamide is about nine times more effective than LH-RH in releasing luteinizing hormone (LH) in immature male rats over a prolonged period.
Polyfluoroalkylamine derivatives of luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone.

2,4-dioxa-spiro[5.5]undec-8-ene derivatives inhibit both 3'-processing and strand transfer reactions catalyzed by human immunodeficiency virus integrase (IN).
Synthesis and HIV-1 integrase inhibitory activity of spiroundecane(ene) derivatives.

1-deoxypaclitaxel derivatives can be synthesized from baccatin VI by selective deacylation and C-13 side chain attachment, and some analogues exhibit tubulin polymerization and cytotoxicity activities comparable to paclitaxel.
Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of 1-Deoxypaclitaxel Analogues.

Dodecyl-alpha-D-maltoside is a non-ionic detergent that stabilizes and enables crystallization of the human nuclear receptor hRARgamma ligand-binding domain by mediating a crystal contact in the coactivator binding region.

5,7-dimethoxy-4-phenylcoumarin produced by Streptomyces aureofaciens CMUAc130 shows in vitro anti-inflammatory activity by modulating iNOS enzyme expression, nitric oxide formation, PGE2 release, and COX-2 expression in LPS-induced murine macrophage RAW 264.7 cells in a concentration-dependent manner.
In vitro anti-inflammatory activity of 4-arylcoumarins from endophytic Streptomyces aureofaciens CMUAc130 in murine macrophage RAW 264.7 cells.

Pipoxide chlorohydrin is a halogenated plant metabolite that shows the greatest affinity and potency for binding to chikungunya virus nsP2pro, as demonstrated by molecular docking, MD simulation, and MMPBSA studies.
Halogenated Secondary Metabolites from Higher Plants: Potent Drug Candidates for Chikungunya Using in silico Approaches.

2'-hydroxy-4'-methoxychalcone suppresses LPS-induced production of nitrite and TNF-α in RAW 264.7 murine macrophage cells by blocking LPS-induced activation of NF-κB and AP-1 signaling.
Inhibition of lipopolysaccharide-induced expression of inducible nitric oxide synthase and tumor necrosis factor-alpha by 2'-hydroxychalcone derivatives in RAW 264.7 cells.

Hydrazides of pyrrol-1-ylbenzoic acid act as highly specific and potent reversible inactivators of copper-dependent amine oxidases, with greater affinity for plasma amine oxidase.

Microporous hexagonal niobium phosphate synthesized with C6–C10 hydrocarbon surfactants by supramolecular templating exhibits strong hydrophilic character and high selectivity (95.3%) for catechol formation from phenol in the presence of methanol and aqueous H2O2.
Microporous niobium phosphates and catalytic properties prepared by a supramolecular templating mechanism.

Adenylyl(2'-5')adenylyl(2'-5')adenosine is a natural 2',5'-oligoadenylate that is less enzymatically stable than synthetic analogs with 3'-terminal acyclic nucleoside residues when treated with NIH 3T3 cell lysates, mouse liver homogenates, or snake venom phosphodiesterase.
A route to 2',5'-oligoadenylates with increased stability towards phosphodiesterases.

Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed one-pot tandem reaction involving Ullmann-type C-N coupling and intramolecular dehydrative cyclization of substituted 2-(2-bromophenyl)-1H-imidazoles and formamide.
Copper-catalyzed tandem Ullmann type C-N coupling and dehydrative cyclization: synthesis of imidazo[1,2-c]quinazolines.
EI-1941-1 is a novel interleukin-1β converting enzyme (ICE) inhibitor isolated from Farrowia sp. E-1941, exhibiting selective inhibition of human recombinant ICE activity with an IC50 of 0.086 μM.

EI-1941-1 and -2, novel interleukin-1 beta converting enzyme inhibitors Produced by Farrowia sp. E-1941. II. Taxonomy of producing strain, fermentation, isolation, physico-chemical properties, and biological properties.

5-vinyldeoxyuridine, when incorporated at the 5'-end of an oligodeoxyribonucleotide, enables non-enzymatic, template-directed, reversible photoligation with cytosine and thymine at the 3'-end upon photoirradiation at 366 nm.
Reversible photoligation of DNA via 5-vinyldeoxyuridine.

Pyroglutamyl leucine is a hepatoprotective peptide identified in wheat gluten hydrolysate that, when administered at 20 mg/kg body weight, reduces serum aspartate and alanine aminotransferases to approximately 30% and 20% of vehicle-treated levels, respectively, in rats with D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis.
Identification of a hepatoprotective peptide in wheat gluten hydrolysate against D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis in rats.
2'(3')-O-Bromoacetyluridine reacts rapidly and selectively with bovine pancreatic ribonuclease A at pH 5.5 and 25°, forming N-3-carboxymethylhistidine-12 ribonuclease A as the product.
The kinetics and specificity of the reaction of 2'(3')-O-bromoacetyluridine with bovine pancreatic ribonuclease A.

19-hydroxyandrosta-4,6-diene-3,17-dione (19OHAD) is a substrate for aromatase whose stereospecificity of oxidation matches that of 19-hydroxyandrost-4-ene-3,17-dione (19OHA), indicating that conformational constraints in 19OHA are not major determinants of aromatase substrate specificity.
Role of substrate conformational features in the stereospecificity of aromatase.

2,3-dihydro-1-butyl-2-thioxoindole-3-one exhibits in vitro antimycobacterial activity against Mycobacterium tuberculosis, M. kansasii, M. fortuitum, M. intracellulare, and M. avium, showing higher activity than isoniazid.
New antimycobacterial 2,3-dihydro-1-alkylindole-2-thiones.

Preincubation of rat hippocampal slices with 0.05–0.5 µM pyroglutamylasparagine amide improves long-term potentiation of focal responses in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synaptic system by facilitating LTP development and increasing its amplitude and duration.
Effect of pyroglutamylasparagine amide on plastic characteristics of synaptic transmission in the hippocampus.
The 6-O-pivaloyl group of methyl 2,6-di-O-pivaloyl-alpha-D-glucopyranoside is selectively removed by esterase II from rabbit serum.
Partial purification of esterases from rabbit serum and their use in regioselective deacylations of sugars.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.

Microporous hexagonal niobium phosphate synthesized with C6–C10 hydrocarbon surfactants by supramolecular templating exhibits strong hydrophilic character and high selectivity (95.3%) for catechol formation from phenol in the presence of methanol and aqueous H2O2.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.
Chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone via kinetic resolution of racemic carboxylic acids: direct access to enantioenriched lactone.

Copper-catalyzed tandem Ullmann type C-N coupling and dehydrative cyclization: synthesis of imidazo[1,2-c]quinazolines.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.
1-(2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl-1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines as potent phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) inhibitors.

Adenylyl(2'-5')adenylyl(2'-5')adenosine is a natural 2',5'-oligoadenylate that is less enzymatically stable than synthetic analogs with 3'-terminal acyclic nucleoside residues when treated with NIH 3T3 cell lysates, mouse liver homogenates, or snake venom phosphodiesterase.
A route to 2',5'-oligoadenylates with increased stability towards phosphodiesterases.

EI-1941-1 and -2, novel interleukin-1 beta converting enzyme inhibitors Produced by Farrowia sp. E-1941. II. Taxonomy of producing strain, fermentation, isolation, physico-chemical properties, and biological properties.
N-(3-cyano-4-methyl-2-pyrrylcarbamoylmethyl)iminodiacetic acid derivatives labeled with technetium-99m have been synthesized and evaluated for hepatobiliary activity in rats.
Evaluation of 99mTc-labeled iminodiacetic acid derivatives of substituted 2-aminopyrroles as hepatobiliary imaging agents in rats II.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be obtained in optically pure form with a yield greater than 99% and enantiomeric excess (ee) of 96% (R) via chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger asymmetric oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone using Candida antarctica lipase B and H2O2.
Chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone via kinetic resolution of racemic carboxylic acids: direct access to enantioenriched lactone.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.

13-hydroxy-14-methylhexadecanoic acid exists as four stereoisomers, and total syntheses of these isomers have been achieved using asymmetric alkynylation, acid-catalyzed lactonization, selective protection of primary alcohol, and Wittig reaction.
Asymmetric syntheses of four stereoisomers of 13-hydroxy-14-methylhexadecanoic acid as potential antibacterial agents.
Pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia under air.
Synthesis of pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives through copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia.

N-hydroxyindoles can be synthesized in high purity and excellent yield (>90%) via lead-promoted intramolecular reductive cyclization of o-nitrobenzyl ketones and aldehydes under transfer hydrogenation conditions.
A general synthesis of N-hydroxyindoles.

A liquid membrane microelectrode using a mixture of benzyldimethylhexadecylammonium cholate and decanol with hexadecyltributylammonium taurocholate/5% hexachlorobenzene + 0.5% bromoacetanilide in o-dichlorobenzene provides a quicker response time and lower drift for bile acid detection.
Two-barrel bile-acids-sensitive microelectrodes based on liquid ion exchanger.
Both (2S,3R)- and (2R,3S)-hydroxycitric acid enantiomers produced by Bacillus megaterium G45C and Streptomyces sp. U121 are optically pure.
Absolute configuration of hydroxycitric acid produced by microorganisms.
Congeners bearing an (E)-cyclooct-2-enylidenemethyl group exhibit more potent type I fatty acid synthase (FAS) inhibitory activity than (S)-3-demethylthiolactomycin with an unnatural configuration.
Synthesis and biological activity of enantiomeric pairs of 5-[(E)-cycloalk-2-enylidenemethyl]thiolactomycin congeners.
1,7-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one can be synthesized in 19% overall yield starting from 4-methoxycinnamic acid via ethyl 5-(4-methoxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-2.4-pentadienoate.
Synthesis of 1,7-bis(4-hydroxypenyl)-4-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one, an antiplatelet diarylheptanoid from Alpinia blepharocalyx K. schum.

[2H8]L-phenylalanine can be incorporated into 2-phenylethyl b-D-glucopyranoside and 2-phenylethanol in Rosa damascena flowers when fed throughout maturation, with the labeling pattern indicating the involvement of phenylpyruvic acid as a major biosynthetic route.

Biogenesis of 2-phenylethanol in rose flowers: incorporation of [2H8]L-phenylalanine into 2-phenylethanol and its beta-D-glucopyranoside during the flower opening of Rosa 'Hoh-Jun' and Rosa damascena Mill.

Pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl isothiocyanate reacts with diamines to form bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas, with yields improved by ultrasound irradiation and reaction times reduced from 7.5 h to 0.75 h compared to conventional methods.
Ultrasound promoted synthesis of bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas.
Gln-Phe is the hydrolysis site for stromelysin-catalyzed cleavage of the substance P peptide substrate, with a kc/Km value of 1700 M⁻¹ s⁻¹.
Substrate specificity of the human matrix metalloproteinase stromelysin and the development of continuous fluorometric assays.

Cys-Glu-Gln-Tyr-Cys (PN7051) exerts its antithrombotic effect by interfering with the docking of Tyr101 into a hydrophobic pocket in the catalytic domain of FVII, thereby disrupting an essential interaction between the second EGF-like and the catalytic domains of FVII.
A cyclic pentapeptide derived from the second EGF-like domain of Factor VII is an inhibitor of tissue factor dependent coagulation and thrombus formation.
Cys-Glu-Gln-Tyr-Cys (PN7051) does not significantly affect the active site of FXa or FVIIa and interferes with FVII/TF/FX complex formation and function.
A cyclic pentapeptide derived from the second EGF-like domain of Factor VII is an inhibitor of tissue factor dependent coagulation and thrombus formation.

Cys-Glu-Gln-Tyr-Cys (PN7051) is a cyclic dodecapeptide that acts as a non-competitive inhibitor of factor Xa (FXa) and inhibits TF-dependent factor Xa activation and plasma coagulation with IC50 values of 10 ± 2 µM and 1.3 ± 0.2 mM, respectively.
A cyclic pentapeptide derived from the second EGF-like domain of Factor VII is an inhibitor of tissue factor dependent coagulation and thrombus formation.
The Gln3-Tyr4 motif of Cys-Glu-Gln-Tyr-Cys (PN7051) is critical for its anticoagulant activity, while Glu2 is not important.
A cyclic pentapeptide derived from the second EGF-like domain of Factor VII is an inhibitor of tissue factor dependent coagulation and thrombus formation.
Tetrahydrofuroyl-l-phenylalanine derivatives have been studied as VLA-4 antagonists using 3-D-QSAR (CoMFA and CoMSIA) models.
3-D-QSAR CoMFA and CoMSIA studies on tetrahydrofuroyl-L-phenylalanine derivatives as VLA-4 antagonists.
The hexapeptide Arg-Glu-Leu-Leu-Lys-Met, when displayed on a filamentous phage, is recognized by autoantibodies from patient ITP-2 that target the GPIIb/IIIa complex.
Characterization of autoantigenic epitopes on platelet glycoprotein IIb/IIIa using random peptide libraries.
Mercaptoacetyl-Phe-Leu inhibits enkephalinase A and soluble aminopeptidase activity, with its inhibitory effect on aminopeptidase being most potent when Phe-Phe is used as the dipeptide moiety.

Pyroglutamyl leucine is a hepatoprotective peptide identified in wheat gluten hydrolysate that, when administered at 20 mg/kg body weight, reduces serum aspartate and alanine aminotransferases to approximately 30% and 20% of vehicle-treated levels, respectively, in rats with D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis.
Identification of a hepatoprotective peptide in wheat gluten hydrolysate against D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis in rats.
2-aminobenzylstatine is a core structure that serves as the basis for designing potent, selective, and noncovalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.
A new structural class of selective and non-covalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.

Application of an external electric field (EEF) to Ser-Glu-Met tripeptide inner salt increases the positive charge density on the hydrogen atoms of the N(+)H3, peptide bond NH, NH2, and COOH groups, and decreases the negative charge density on the oxygen atoms of the peptide bond carbonyl groups.
Effect of the external electric field on selected tripeptides.

When added before cytochalasin B, N-Formyl-norleucine-leucyl-phenylalanine chloromethyl ketone (f Nle-Leu-Phe-COCH2Cl) produces a neutrophil response that is three to four times greater at three tested time points than that to the free acid under the same conditions.
N alpha-formyl-norleucyl-leucyl-phenylalanyl chloromethylketone. A possible covalent agonist of the N alpha-formyl-methionyl-peptide receptor.

In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.
Tetrahydrofuroyl-l-phenylalanine derivatives act as VLA-4 antagonists and can be studied for activity using 3-D-QSAR models such as CoMFA and CoMSIA.
3-D-QSAR CoMFA and CoMSIA studies on tetrahydrofuroyl-L-phenylalanine derivatives as VLA-4 antagonists.

The antioxidant tetrapeptide O-methyl-l-tyrosinyl-gamma-l-glutamyl-l-cysteinylglycine (UPF1) partially counteracts the e-cigarette liquid-induced alterations in the metabolome of human bronchial epithelial cells, with maximum effect at the 5th hour of exposure.
E-Cigarette Affects the Metabolome of Primary Normal Human Bronchial Epithelial Cells.

Pretreatment with N-Formyl-norleucine-leucyl-phenylalanine chloromethyl ketone (f Nle-Leu-Phe-COCH2Cl) causes a greater reduction in neutrophil binding sites for N-Formylmethionine-leucyl-phenylalanine-[3H]Phe than the free acid, indicating higher potency in down-regulating chemotaxis responsiveness.
N alpha-formyl-norleucyl-leucyl-phenylalanyl chloromethylketone. A possible covalent agonist of the N alpha-formyl-methionyl-peptide receptor.

O-methyl-L-tyrosinyl-gamma-L-glutamyl-L-cysteinylglycine (UPF1) accelerates recovery of human bronchial epithelial cells from cigarette smoke condensate exposure and restores redox pathway and energy production metabolites more rapidly than N-acetylcysteine.
Alterations of bronchial epithelial metabolome by cigarette smoke are reversible by an antioxidant, O-methyl-L-tyrosinyl-γ-L-glutamyl-L-cysteinylglycine.

Cys-Glu-Gln-Tyr-Cys (PN7051) inhibits acute thrombus formation in an ex vivo human thrombosis model with IC50 values between 0.3 and 0.7 mM for fibrin deposition, platelet-fibrin adhesion, platelet-thrombus formation, and thrombin-antithrombin complex formation.
A cyclic pentapeptide derived from the second EGF-like domain of Factor VII is an inhibitor of tissue factor dependent coagulation and thrombus formation.

N-(N-hydroxysulfamoyl)phenylalanine is a transition state analogue inhibitor of carboxypeptidase A, binding in a manner reminiscent of a transition state and exhibiting a competitive inhibition with a Ki in the micromolar range.
Sulfamide derivatives as transition state analogue inhibitors for carboxypeptidase A.

Intracerebroventricular injection of N-[(RS)-2-carboxy-3-phenylpropanoyl]-L-leucine in mice produces high potentiation of the analgesic effect of D-Ala2-Met-enkephalin, indicating in vivo inhibition of enkephalinase.

Pyroglutamyl leucine is a hepatoprotective peptide identified in wheat gluten hydrolysate that, when administered at 20 mg/kg body weight, reduces serum aspartate and alanine aminotransferases to approximately 30% and 20% of vehicle-treated levels, respectively, in rats with D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.

1,7-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one can be synthesized in 19% overall yield starting from 4-methoxycinnamic acid via ethyl 5-(4-methoxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-2.4-pentadienoate.

Disodium 1-[2-(carboxylato)pyrrolidin-1-yl]diazen-1-ium-1,2-diolate (PROLI/NO) is a low molecular weight nitric oxide donor that, when incorporated into electrospun polymer fibers, enables nitric oxide release upon immersion in aqueous solutions at physiological pH (7.4, 37°C).

1-{(1S)-2-[amino]-1-[3-(trifluoromethoxy)phenyl]ethyl}cyclohexanol analogues are designed to improve in vitro metabolic stability and brain penetration.
Discovery of WAY-260022, a Potent and Selective Inhibitor of the Norepinephrine Transporter.

Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed one-pot tandem reaction involving Ullmann-type C-N coupling and intramolecular dehydrative cyclization of substituted 2-(2-bromophenyl)-1H-imidazoles and formamide.
Copper-catalyzed tandem Ullmann type C-N coupling and dehydrative cyclization: synthesis of imidazo[1,2-c]quinazolines.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.

Adenylyl(2'-5')adenylyl(2'-5')adenosine is a natural 2',5'-oligoadenylate that is less enzymatically stable than synthetic analogs with 3'-terminal acyclic nucleoside residues when treated with NIH 3T3 cell lysates, mouse liver homogenates, or snake venom phosphodiesterase.
A route to 2',5'-oligoadenylates with increased stability towards phosphodiesterases.
Pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia under air.
Synthesis of pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives through copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia.
3,24-bis(3-bromophenoxy)cholane undergoes palladium-catalyzed amination with polyamines and polyoxadiamines to form macrocycles containing steroidal and polyamine moieties, yielding 38–65%.
Synthesis of nitrogen- and oxygen-containing macrocycles--derivatives of lithocholic Acid.

Trifluoromethylphenylimidazole (TRIM) selectively inhibits capacitative calcium entry (CCE) in mouse anococcygeus smooth muscle, producing a concentration-dependent relaxation of thapsigargin-induced tone with an EC50 of 42 µM.
Selective inhibition of thapsigargin-induced contraction and capacitative calcium entry in mouse anococcygeus by trifluoromethylphenylimidazole (TRIM).
1-(Benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-(4-chlorophenyl)ethanol acts as a positive allosteric modulator (PAM) of the calcium-sensing receptor (CaSR) in a functional cell-based assay.
Discovery of a class of calcium sensing receptor positive allosteric modulators; 1-(benzothiazol-2-yl)-1-phenylethanols.

spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one can be synthesized via a formal 1,6-conjugated addition-mediated [2+1] annulation using p-quinone methides and sulfur ylides, resulting in a highly diastereoselective and scalable process with good functional group tolerance and no need for metals or bases.
1,6-Conjugated Addition-Mediated [2+1] Annulation: Approach to Spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one.

1-deoxypaclitaxel derivatives can be synthesized from baccatin VI by selective deacylation and C-13 side chain attachment, and some analogues exhibit tubulin polymerization and cytotoxicity activities comparable to paclitaxel.
Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of 1-Deoxypaclitaxel Analogues.

2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol is a novel chiral ligand used to synthesize dinuclear platinum(II) complexes with demonstrated in vitro cytotoxicity against human HEPG-2, A549, and HCT-116 cell lines.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.

N-hydroxyindoles can be synthesized in high purity and excellent yield (>90%) via lead-promoted intramolecular reductive cyclization of o-nitrobenzyl ketones and aldehydes under transfer hydrogenation conditions.
A general synthesis of N-hydroxyindoles.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

Preincubation of rat hippocampal slices with 0.05–0.5 µM pyroglutamylasparagine amide improves long-term potentiation of focal responses in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synaptic system by facilitating LTP development and increasing its amplitude and duration.
Effect of pyroglutamylasparagine amide on plastic characteristics of synaptic transmission in the hippocampus.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.
1-(2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl-1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines as potent phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) inhibitors.

16-(3-methoxyphenyl)-9,10,11,12,13,16-hexahydro-15H-benzo [5',6']chromeno [2',3':4,5]pyrimido [1,2-a]azepin-15-imine is a benzochromene derivative identified as a potential multitarget-directed ligand for Alzheimer's disease therapy, acting as a human acetylcholinesterase inhibitor with an IC50 of 0.36 ± 0.02 mM, exhibiting strong antioxidant activity (3.61 mmol Troloxe equivalents), and moderate beta-amyloid aggregation inhibition (40.3%).
Synthesis and Biological Assessment of Racemic Benzochromenopyrimidinimines as Antioxidant, Cholinesterase, and Aβ1-42 Aggregation Inhibitors for Alzheimer's Disease Therapy.

Microporous hexagonal niobium phosphate synthesized with C6–C10 hydrocarbon surfactants by supramolecular templating exhibits strong hydrophilic character and high selectivity (95.3%) for catechol formation from phenol in the presence of methanol and aqueous H2O2.
Microporous niobium phosphates and catalytic properties prepared by a supramolecular templating mechanism.

(3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE) inhibits the development of type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice by suppressing delayed-type hypersensitivity to type II collagen and reducing arthritis incidence and severity.
Effect of (3,5,6-trimethylpyrazin-2-yl)methyl 2-[4-(2-methylpropyl)phenyl]propanoate (ITE), a newly developed anti-inflammatory drug, on type II collagen-induced arthritis in mice.

[2H8]L-phenylalanine can be incorporated into 2-phenylethyl b-D-glucopyranoside and 2-phenylethanol in Rosa damascena flowers when fed throughout maturation, with the labeling pattern indicating the involvement of phenylpyruvic acid as a major biosynthetic route.

Biogenesis of 2-phenylethanol in rose flowers: incorporation of [2H8]L-phenylalanine into 2-phenylethanol and its beta-D-glucopyranoside during the flower opening of Rosa 'Hoh-Jun' and Rosa damascena Mill.
Triphenylmethyldiethylene glycol acts as a potent and competitive inhibitor of bilirubin glucuronidation, with IC50 and Ki values similar to its hydrocarbon analog.
Mechanism of inhibition of rat liver bilirubin UDP-glucuronosyltransferase by triphenylalkyl derivatives.
Isocoumarin phosphorus analogues (phosphaisocoumarins) can act as reversible competitive inhibitors of pancreatic cholesterol esterase (CEase), with some compounds showing potent inhibitory activity.
Phosphaisocoumarins as a new class of potent inhibitors for pancreatic cholesterol esterase.

5-benzylpyridone derivatives of tripeptide-based inhibitors have been developed as non-peptidic human leukocyte elastase (HLE) inhibitors, with a 5-benzyl substitution on the pyridone ring improving potency by forming a lipophilic interaction with the enzyme S2 pocket.
Anionic- and lipophilic-mediated surface binding inhibitors of human leukocyte elastase.
Benzimidazole-1,2,3-triazole compounds can be synthesized via a click reaction in DMF/H2O and exhibit antidiabetic activity, including inhibition of α-amylase and α-glucosidase.
Synthesis and antidiabetic evaluation of benzimidazole-tethered 1,2,3-triazoles.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.
Triphenylmethanol alone does not inhibit the glucuronidation of 4-methylumbelliferone by human liver recombinant UGT1*6.
Mechanism of inhibition of rat liver bilirubin UDP-glucuronosyltransferase by triphenylalkyl derivatives.

Triphenylethanamine (TPE) is a series of CETP inhibitors with higher potency than DPPE analogs, and structure-activity relationship studies in the TPE series informed the design of more potent compounds.
Discovery of a Lead Triphenylethanamine Cholesterol Ester Transfer Protein (CETP) Inhibitor.

N-(Dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol serves as a lead compound for developing diagnostic compounds for tracking mutant beta-galactosidase and aberrant GM1 gangliosides by live cell imaging.
A fluorescent probe for GM1 gangliosidosis related β-galactosidase: N-(dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol.

Trimethylhydroquinone derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated for anti-lipid peroxidation activity, inhibition of rat basophilic leukemia-1 (RBL-1) cell 5-lipoxygenase, and reduction of 48 h homologous passive cutaneous anaphylaxis (PCA) activity in rats.

N3-(fluoromethyl)-thymidine, a thymidine analog with a fluoroalkyl group at the N3 position, is not degraded by recombinant E. coli thymidylate phosphorylase (TP) during 60-minute incubations.
Thymidine phosphorylase inhibitors: recent developments and potential therapeutic applications.
N-formyl-N-hydroxyphenylalanine N-methylamide (HCONOH-b-Phe-NHMe) is a potent competitive transglutaminase 2 (TLN) inhibitor with a Ki value of 1.66 ± 0.05 mM.

N-((2-methyl-[1,1'-biphenyl]-3-yl)methyl)pyrimidin-2-amine serves as a basic skeletal structure for the design of small molecule inhibitors targeting the PD-1/PD-L1 axis.
Design, synthesis and biological evaluation of 2-methyl-(1,1'-biphenyl)-pyrimidine conjugates.
2-deoxy-2-fluoro-α-D-glucopyranosyl dibenzyl phosphate does not function as a covalent inactivator of its cognate α-glycosidase.

Evaluation of 99mTc-labeled iminodiacetic acid derivatives of substituted 2-aminopyrroles as hepatobiliary imaging agents in rats II.

N-hydroxyindoles can be synthesized in high purity and excellent yield (>90%) via lead-promoted intramolecular reductive cyclization of o-nitrobenzyl ketones and aldehydes under transfer hydrogenation conditions.
A general synthesis of N-hydroxyindoles.

Pyroglutamyl leucine is a hepatoprotective peptide identified in wheat gluten hydrolysate that, when administered at 20 mg/kg body weight, reduces serum aspartate and alanine aminotransferases to approximately 30% and 20% of vehicle-treated levels, respectively, in rats with D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis.
Identification of a hepatoprotective peptide in wheat gluten hydrolysate against D-galactosamine-induced acute hepatitis in rats.
N6-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-8-(2-thienyl)adenosine inhibits trypanosomal glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase with a Ki of 9 µM and shows marginal selectivity for the parasite enzyme.
Synthesis and structure-activity relationships of adenosine analogs as inhibitors of trypanosomal glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase. Modifications at positions 5' and 8.

(2S)-1,2-di-O-[(9Z,12Z,15Z)-octadeca-9,12,15-trienoyl]-3-O-β-D-galactopyranosyl glycerol is a galactolipid isolated from Rosa canina that acts as an antiinflammatory agent by inhibiting chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro, without being related to toxicity.
An antiinflammatory galactolipid from rose hip (Rosa canina) that inhibits chemotaxis of human peripheral blood neutrophils in vitro.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one can be synthesized via a formal 1,6-conjugated addition-mediated [2+1] annulation using p-quinone methides and sulfur ylides, resulting in a highly diastereoselective and scalable process with good functional group tolerance and no need for metals or bases.
1,6-Conjugated Addition-Mediated [2+1] Annulation: Approach to Spiro[2.5]octa-4,7-dien-6-one.

Pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl isothiocyanate reacts with diamines to form bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas, with yields improved by ultrasound irradiation and reaction times reduced from 7.5 h to 0.75 h compared to conventional methods.
Ultrasound promoted synthesis of bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas.

Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed one-pot tandem reaction involving Ullmann-type C-N coupling and intramolecular dehydrative cyclization of substituted 2-(2-bromophenyl)-1H-imidazoles and formamide.
Copper-catalyzed tandem Ullmann type C-N coupling and dehydrative cyclization: synthesis of imidazo[1,2-c]quinazolines.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.

Pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia under air.
Synthesis of pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives through copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia.

Microporous hexagonal niobium phosphate synthesized with C6–C10 hydrocarbon surfactants by supramolecular templating exhibits strong hydrophilic character and high selectivity (95.3%) for catechol formation from phenol in the presence of methanol and aqueous H2O2.
Microporous niobium phosphates and catalytic properties prepared by a supramolecular templating mechanism.

2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol is a novel chiral ligand used to synthesize dinuclear platinum(II) complexes with demonstrated in vitro cytotoxicity against human HEPG-2, A549, and HCT-116 cell lines.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.
N-(3-cyano-4-methyl-2-pyrrylcarbamoylmethyl)iminodiacetic acid derivatives labeled with technetium-99m have been synthesized and evaluated for hepatobiliary activity in rats.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.
1-(2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl-1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines as potent phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) inhibitors.

N-(Dansylamino)hexylaminocarbonylpentyl-1,5-dideoxy-1,5-imino-D-galactitol serves as a lead compound for developing diagnostic compounds for tracking mutant beta-galactosidase and aberrant GM1 gangliosides by live cell imaging.

Bis-chelated gold(I) diphosphine complexes, such as [Au(d2pypp)2]Cl, selectively induce apoptosis in breast cancer cells at submicromolar concentrations by targeting mitochondria and inhibiting the thioredoxin/thioredoxin reductase system.
A gold(I) phosphine complex selectively induces apoptosis in breast cancer cells: implications for anticancer therapeutics targeted to mitochondria.

2-benzoyl-6-methylpyridine thiosemicarbazone (6-MeBpT) is a novel thiosemicarbazone iron chelator whose analogues with a single methyl substituent on the pyridine ring exhibit the highest anti-proliferative activity among tested compounds.
Synthesis and biological evaluation of substituted 2-benzoylpyridine thiosemicarbazones: novel structure-activity relationships underpinning their anti-proliferative and chelation efficacy.
The hexapeptide Arg-Glu-Leu-Leu-Lys-Met, when displayed on a filamentous phage, is recognized by autoantibodies from patient ITP-2 that target the GPIIb/IIIa complex.
Characterization of autoantigenic epitopes on platelet glycoprotein IIb/IIIa using random peptide libraries.

Methyl substitution of 1,10-phenanthroline to 4,7-dimethyl-1,10-phenanthroline in oxovanadium complexes enhances anti-leukemic activity, as seen in bis(4,7-dimethyl-1,10-phenanthroline)sulfatooxovanadium(IV) which induces reactive oxygen species production, G1-arrest, and inhibits clonogenic growth in human leukemia cells at nanomolar concentrations.

1,2,3,4-tetrahydrochromeno[3,2-c]pyridine-10-one derivatives have been synthesized and shown to exhibit multitargeting inhibitory activity against enzymes and processes involved in Alzheimer's disease, including acetylcholinesterase, butyrylcholinesterase, monoamine oxidases A and B, beta-amyloid aggregation, and reactive oxygen species production.

(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one (TIM) protects mouse photoreceptor-derived 661W cells from lipoteichoic acid-induced toxicity by decreasing intracellular ROS and MDA levels, enhancing SOD activity and GSH levels, and increasing Nrf2 protein expression.
(3R)-5,6,7-trihydroxy-3-isopropyl-3-methylisochroman-1-one alleviates lipoteichoic acid-induced photoreceptor cell damage.
Pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia under air.

Preincubation of rat hippocampal slices with 0.05–0.5 µM pyroglutamylasparagine amide improves long-term potentiation of focal responses in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synaptic system by facilitating LTP development and increasing its amplitude and duration.

Synthesis and Biological Assessment of Racemic Benzochromenopyrimidinimines as Antioxidant, Cholinesterase, and Aβ1-42 Aggregation Inhibitors for Alzheimer's Disease Therapy.

MitoEbselen-2 is a mitochondria-targeted mimic of glutathione peroxidase that reduces lipid hydroperoxides, prevents apoptotic cell death, and increases survival in mice exposed to whole-body gamma irradiation when administered 24 hours postirradiation.

Ethyl-4-hydroxy-3-methoxycinnamic acid protects neurons against amyloid b-peptide(1-42)-induced toxicity by up-regulating heme oxygenase-1 and heat shock protein 72, and down-regulating inducible nitric oxide synthase.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.

Ammonium acetate-acetic acid acts as a catalyst for the condensation of ketones with ethyl cyanoacetate to produce ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetate under ultrasound irradiation.

(2R)-7-(3-[2-chloro-4-(4-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]propoxy)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acid exhibits substantial antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic activities when orally administered in db/db mouse type 2 diabetes, Syrian hamster lipid, and dog lipid models.

(E)-2-(2-(4-(dimethylamino)styryl)-6-methyl-4H-pyran-4-ylidene) malononitrile (PAD-1) is a smart dicynomethylene-4H-pyran fluorophore that rapidly enters the brain after intravenous injection and shows a turn-on fluorescence effect upon binding to aggregated beta-amyloid fibrils.

3-amino-N-(4-(4-(1,2-benzisothiazol-3-yl)-1-piperazinyl)butyl)-2 -pyridinecarboxamide is a heterocyclic carboxamide analogue that exhibits potent in vivo antipsychotic activity comparable to 1192U90 in the mouse climbing assay.

6-methoxy-2-[4-(2-methoxyphenyl)piperazin-1-yl]-9H-xanthen-9-one (HBK-11) is a xanthone derivative with potential antidepressant activity, reducing immobility time by 32% to 50% at 20 mg/kg in mice at 10 mg/kg in the FST assay.

N-[4-[4-(2,3-dichlorophenyl)piperazin-1-yl]butyl]-3-methoxybenzamide is a high-affinity D(3) receptor ligand with selectivity over D(2), D(4), 5-HT(1A), and alpha(1) receptors, and is considered a valuable candidate for PET imaging due to its affinity, lipophilicity, and suitability for O-methyl (11)C labeling.

4-chloro-3-nitro-N-butylbenzenesulfonamide (SMD2) alters the steady-state inactivation properties of K V 3.1 channels, increasing V1/2 and K values to -30.3 ± 1.3 mV and 6 ± 0.8 mV, respectively, in the presence of the blocker compared to control values of -22.9 ± 1.5 mV and 5.3 ± 0.9 mV.
4-Chloro-3-nitro-N-butylbenzenesulfonamide acts on KV3.1 channels by an open-channel blocker mechanism.

16-(3-methoxyphenyl)-9,10,11,12,13,16-hexahydro-15H-benzo [5',6']chromeno [2',3':4,5]pyrimido [1,2-a]azepin-15-imine is a benzochromene derivative identified as a potential multitarget-directed ligand for Alzheimer's disease therapy, acting as a human acetylcholinesterase inhibitor with an IC50 of 0.36 ± 0.02 mM, exhibiting strong antioxidant activity (3.61 mmol Troloxe equivalents), and moderate beta-amyloid aggregation inhibition (40.3%).
Synthesis and Biological Assessment of Racemic Benzochromenopyrimidinimines as Antioxidant, Cholinesterase, and Aβ1-42 Aggregation Inhibitors for Alzheimer's Disease Therapy.
Cyclopropeniminium ions can be ligated with bioorthogonal phosphines via formal 1,2-addition to a pi-system in aqueous solution.
Cyclopropeniminium Ions Exhibit Unique Reactivity Profiles with Bioorthogonal Phosphines.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.
2-aminobenzylstatine is a core structure that serves as the basis for designing potent, selective, and noncovalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.
A new structural class of selective and non-covalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.

m-phenylenediacetic acid reacts with tetranuclear copper(II) complexes to yield both discrete tetracopper cationic complexes and cluster polymers, such as [Cu4{mu-(alpha-D-Glc-1P)}2(mu-m-C6H4(CH2COO)2)(bpy)4](NO3)2 and {[Cu4{mu-(alpha-D-Glc-1P)}2(mu-m-C6H4(CH2COO)2)(bpy)4](NO3)2}n.
Tetranuclear copper(II) complexes bridged by alpha-D-glucose-1-phosphate and incorporation of sugar acids through the Cu4 core structural changes.
Isocoumarin phosphorus analogues (phosphaisocoumarins) can act as reversible competitive inhibitors of pancreatic cholesterol esterase (CEase), with some compounds showing potent inhibitory activity.
Phosphaisocoumarins as a new class of potent inhibitors for pancreatic cholesterol esterase.

1,3-phenylenediacetic acid was used as a P4 linker in macrocyclic inhibitors, resulting in compounds with improved binding affinity (K i * = 0.44 μM) compared to a glutaric acid linker in HCV protease inhibitors.
Macrocyclic inhibitors of HCV NS3 protease.

Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed one-pot tandem reaction involving Ullmann-type C-N coupling and intramolecular dehydrative cyclization of substituted 2-(2-bromophenyl)-1H-imidazoles and formamide.
Copper-catalyzed tandem Ullmann type C-N coupling and dehydrative cyclization: synthesis of imidazo[1,2-c]quinazolines.

N-(N-hydroxysulfamoyl)phenylalanine is a transition state analogue inhibitor of carboxypeptidase A, binding in a manner reminiscent of a transition state and exhibiting a competitive inhibition with a Ki in the micromolar range.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.

1,7-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one can be synthesized in 19% overall yield starting from 4-methoxycinnamic acid via ethyl 5-(4-methoxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-2.4-pentadienoate.

L-glutamic acid-gamma-hydrazide induces GABA accumulation in the substantia nigra of rats, and this effect is reduced on the side opposite to the posterior hypothalamic hemisection.
Evidence for a nerve impulse-dependent GABA accumulation in the substantia nigra after treatment with gamma-glutamylhydrazide.

A nonintoxicating oral dose of 1,3-Butanediol at 4 grams per kilogram administered after ethanol elimination is effective in suppressing the tremulous and convulsive components of ethanol withdrawal syndrome in rats for 1 to 5 hours.
Suppression by 1,3-butanediol of the ethanol withdrawal syndrome in rats.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.
N-(3-cyano-4-methyl-2-pyrrylcarbamoylmethyl)iminodiacetic acid derivatives labeled with technetium-99m have been synthesized and evaluated for hepatobiliary activity in rats.
Evaluation of 99mTc-labeled iminodiacetic acid derivatives of substituted 2-aminopyrroles as hepatobiliary imaging agents in rats II.

His-Gly-Ile, a peptide from soybean 11S glycinin hydrolysate, produces a less reduction in intracellular Ca(2+) concentration compared to other vasoactive peptides in angiotensin II-stimulated vascular smooth muscle cells.
Identification of peptides from soybean protein, glycinin, possessing suppression of intracellular Ca2+ concentration in vascular smooth muscle cells.

Preincubation of rat hippocampal slices with 0.05–0.5 µM pyroglutamylasparagine amide improves long-term potentiation of focal responses in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synaptic system by facilitating LTP development and increasing its amplitude and duration.
Effect of pyroglutamylasparagine amide on plastic characteristics of synaptic transmission in the hippocampus.
Ala-Ser-Leu is a novel angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitory peptide identified from silkworm pupa (Bombyx mori) protein hydrolysate, with an IC50 value of 102.15 µM.

A novel angiotensin-І converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitory peptide from gastrointestinal protease hydrolysate of silkworm pupa (Bombyx mori) protein: Biochemical characterization and molecular docking study.

Microporous hexagonal niobium phosphate synthesized with C6–C10 hydrocarbon surfactants by supramolecular templating exhibits strong hydrophilic character and high selectivity (95.3%) for catechol formation from phenol in the presence of methanol and aqueous H2O2.
Microporous niobium phosphates and catalytic properties prepared by a supramolecular templating mechanism.

[2H8]L-phenylalanine can be incorporated into 2-phenylethyl b-D-glucopyranoside and 2-phenylethanol in Rosa damascena flowers when fed throughout maturation, with the labeling pattern indicating the involvement of phenylpyruvic acid as a major biosynthetic route.

Biogenesis of 2-phenylethanol in rose flowers: incorporation of [2H8]L-phenylalanine into 2-phenylethanol and its beta-D-glucopyranoside during the flower opening of Rosa 'Hoh-Jun' and Rosa damascena Mill.

The presence of succinate acetate in vivo proton MR spectroscopy indicates the presence of degenerating cysticerci in the brain, distinguishing it from anaerobic brain abscesses where acetate is present alone or at higher concentrations than succinate.
Higher succinate than acetate levels differentiate cerebral degenerating cysticerci from anaerobic abscesses on in-vivo proton MR spectroscopy.

(2R,4R)-2,4-dihydroxypentanoic acid is an ether-linked component of bacterial glycans, specifically identified in the O-polysaccharide of Providencia alcalifaciens O31, where it forms an ether linkage with d-mannose at the O-2 and O-4 positions.
Structure of the O-polysaccharide from the lipopolysaccharide of Providencia alcalifaciens O31 containing an ether of D-mannose with (2R,4R)-2,4-dihydroxypentanoic acid.
2-aminobenzylstatine is a core structure that serves as the basis for designing potent, selective, and noncovalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.
A new structural class of selective and non-covalent inhibitors of the chymotrypsin-like activity of the 20S proteasome.
(2R,4R)-2,4-dihydroxypentanoic acid forms an ether linkage with D-mannose in the O-polysaccharide of P. alcalifaciens O31.
Structure of the O-polysaccharide from the lipopolysaccharide of Providencia alcalifaciens O31 containing an ether of D-mannose with (2R,4R)-2,4-dihydroxypentanoic acid.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.
Chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of 4-methylcyclohexanone via kinetic resolution of racemic carboxylic acids: direct access to enantioenriched lactone.

13-hydroxy-14-methylhexadecanoic acid exists as four stereoisomers, and total syntheses of these isomers have been achieved using asymmetric alkynylation, acid-catalyzed lactonization, selective protection of primary alcohol, and Wittig reaction.
Asymmetric syntheses of four stereoisomers of 13-hydroxy-14-methylhexadecanoic acid as potential antibacterial agents.

Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed one-pot tandem reaction involving Ullmann-type C-N coupling and intramolecular dehydrative cyclization of substituted 2-(2-bromophenyl)-1H-imidazoles and formamide.
Copper-catalyzed tandem Ullmann type C-N coupling and dehydrative cyclization: synthesis of imidazo[1,2-c]quinazolines.
1,7-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one can be synthesized in 19% overall yield starting from 4-methoxycinnamic acid via ethyl 5-(4-methoxyphenyl)-3-hydroxy-2.4-pentadienoate.
Synthesis of 1,7-bis(4-hydroxypenyl)-4-hydroxy-1,3-heptadiene-5-one, an antiplatelet diarylheptanoid from Alpinia blepharocalyx K. schum.

Pivaloylvaline is hydrolyzed by human butyrylcholinesterase (hBuChE) in a Michaelis-Menten-like reaction with a Vmax of approximately 40 µM min⁻¹ and a Km within the 0.001–3.000 mM concentration range.
Phenyl valerate esterase activity of human butyrylcholinesterase.
Palmitic-acid-hydroxy-stearic-acid (9-PAHSA) is a branched fatty acid ester of a hydroxyl fatty acid that concentration-dependently activates FFA4 in mammalian adipose tissue.
Discovery of a class of endogenous mammalian lipids with anti-diabetic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Palmitic-acid-hydroxy-stearic-acid (9-PAHSA) shares anti-diabetic and anti-inflammatory effects with the FFA4 agonists DHA and GW9508.
Discovery of a class of endogenous mammalian lipids with anti-diabetic and anti-inflammatory effects.
The subcellular localization of ␣-oxidation for both R- and S-isomers of 3-methylhexadecanoic acid is peroxisomal in rat liver.
Alpha-oxidation of 3-methyl-substituted fatty acids in rat liver.
3-methylhexadecanoic acid undergoes ␣-oxidation in rat liver and hepatocytes, with both R- and S-isomers showing comparable rates of ␣-oxidation in intact hepatocytes and rat liver homogenates.
Alpha-oxidation of 3-methyl-substituted fatty acids in rat liver.

Pyrene octanoate, a long chain pyrenyl fatty acid, incorporates efficiently into phospholipids, mainly phosphatidylcholine and phosphatidylethanolamine, and neutral lipids such as di- and triglycerides and cholesterol esters in BHK fibroblasts.
Metabolism of pyrenyl fatty acids in baby hamster kidney fibroblasts. Effect of the acyl chain length.
3-methylmargaric acid serves as a substrate for a-oxidation and c-u-oxidation reactions in rat hepatocytes, liver homogenates, and microsomal fractions.
Alpha-oxidation of 3-methyl-substituted fatty acids in rat liver.
(2R)-7-(3-[2-chloro-4-(4-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]propoxy)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acid is a potent, structurally novel, selective PPARalpha/gamma dual agonist.
(2R)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acids: discovery of novel PPARalpha/gamma dual agonists as antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic agents.

6,7-(methylenedioxy)-1-octyl-4-quinolone-3-carboxylic acid (Cpd1) effectively prevents and treats malignant hyperthermia and heat stroke in mouse models by reducing resting intracellular Ca2+, inhibiting halothane- and isoflurane-induced Ca2+ release, suppressing caffeine-induced contracture, reducing sarcolemmal cation influx, and reversing fulminant MH crises and heat stroke.
A novel RyR1-selective inhibitor prevents and rescues sudden death in mouse models of malignant hyperthermia and heat stroke.

3-hydroxytridecenoic acid (3HTDA) at concentrations up to 100 μM does not significantly alter liver mitochondrial respiratory parameters such as resting (state 4) and respiratory control ratio (RCR) when glutamate plus malate or succinate are used as substrates.

Uncoupling, metabolic inhibition and induction of mitochondrial permeability transition in rat liver mitochondria caused by the major long-chain hydroxyl monocarboxylic fatty acids accumulating in LCHAD deficiency.
ABHD6 is a key regulator of 1-methyl-2-pentadecanoyl-glycerol (1-MAG) hydrolysis in visceral and brown adipose tissues.
Development of small-molecule inhibitors targeting adipose triglyceride lipase.
3-hydroxytridecenoic acid at concentrations of 10–30 μM significantly reduces mitochondrial Ca 2+ retention capacity in liver mitochondria, and this effect is completely prevented by cyclosporin A.

Uncoupling, metabolic inhibition and induction of mitochondrial permeability transition in rat liver mitochondria caused by the major long-chain hydroxyl monocarboxylic fatty acids accumulating in LCHAD deficiency.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.
Topically applied liposome-encapsulated copper palmitate provides significant protection against both Photofrin- and aminolevulinic acid-induced protoporphyrin IX photosensitivity in animal skin.
Topical use of liposomal copper palmitate formulation blocks porphyrin-induced photosensitivity in rats.

5-[3-(3-(4-phenoxy-2-propylphenoxy)propoxy)phenyl]-2,4-thiazolidinedione sodium salt is a dual ␣/␥ peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor (PPAR) agonist with potential use in the treatment of diabetic patients.
Fine tuning of PPAR ligands for type 2 diabetes and metabolic syndrome.

13-hydroxy-14-methylhexadecanoic acid exists as four stereoisomers, and total syntheses of these isomers have been achieved using asymmetric alkynylation, acid-catalyzed lactonization, selective protection of primary alcohol, and Wittig reaction.
Asymmetric syntheses of four stereoisomers of 13-hydroxy-14-methylhexadecanoic acid as potential antibacterial agents.
3-hydroxytridecenoic acid does not alter mitochondrial swelling in liver mitochondria under the tested conditions.

Uncoupling, metabolic inhibition and induction of mitochondrial permeability transition in rat liver mitochondria caused by the major long-chain hydroxyl monocarboxylic fatty acids accumulating in LCHAD deficiency.
(2R)-7-(3-[2-chloro-4-(4-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]propoxy)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acid is a potent, structurally novel, selective PPARalpha/gamma dual agonist.
Fine tuning of PPAR ligands for type 2 diabetes and metabolic syndrome.
Arachidic acid ethyl ester, a saturated fatty acid ethyl ester, induces sustained, concentration-dependent increases in cytosolic Ca 2؉ and causes cell death in pancreatic acinar cells.
Ethanol toxicity in pancreatic acinar cells: mediation by nonoxidative fatty acid metabolites.
Arachidic acid ethyl ester (20:0) elevates intracellular Ca 2+ in a concentration-dependent manner, contributing to ethanol-induced pancreatic injury.
Ethanol toxicity in pancreatic acinar cells: mediation by nonoxidative fatty acid metabolites.

(2R)-7-(3-[2-chloro-4-(4-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]propoxy)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acid exhibits substantial antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic activities when orally administered in db/db mouse type 2 diabetes, Syrian hamster lipid, and dog lipid models.
(2R)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acids: discovery of novel PPARalpha/gamma dual agonists as antihyperglycemic and hypolipidemic agents.

Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed one-pot tandem reaction involving Ullmann-type C-N coupling and intramolecular dehydrative cyclization of substituted 2-(2-bromophenyl)-1H-imidazoles and formamide.
Copper-catalyzed tandem Ullmann type C-N coupling and dehydrative cyclization: synthesis of imidazo[1,2-c]quinazolines.

2-(((1R,2R)-2-aminocyclohexylamino)methyl)phenol is a novel chiral ligand used to synthesize dinuclear platinum(II) complexes with demonstrated in vitro cytotoxicity against human HEPG-2, A549, and HCT-116 cell lines.
Design, synthesis and in vitro cytotoxicity of novel dinuclear platinum(II) complexes.
Chalconeimine derivatives have been studied for their in vitro and molecular docking interactions with alpha-glucosidase (PDB ID: 2ZEO), a target associated with antioxidant activity.
In vitro and molecular docking analysis of chalconeimine derivatives with α-glucosidase.

5-vinyldeoxyuridine, when incorporated at the 5'-end of an oligodeoxyribonucleotide, enables non-enzymatic, template-directed, reversible photoligation with cytosine and thymine at the 3'-end upon photoirradiation at 366 nm.
Reversible photoligation of DNA via 5-vinyldeoxyuridine.
2-iminocoumarin-3-carboxamide derivatives are small molecule fluorophores that are cell membrane permeable, exhibit low cytotoxicity, and can selectively stain organelles in living cells.
Cell-permeable iminocoumarine-based fluorescent dyes for mitochondria.

Adenylyl(2'-5')adenylyl(2'-5')adenosine is a natural 2',5'-oligoadenylate that is less enzymatically stable than synthetic analogs with 3'-terminal acyclic nucleoside residues when treated with NIH 3T3 cell lysates, mouse liver homogenates, or snake venom phosphodiesterase.
A route to 2',5'-oligoadenylates with increased stability towards phosphodiesterases.

Miliacin is a plant triterpenoid that protects splenocytes from dexamethasone-induced apoptosis by decreasing DNA fragmentation, likely through blockade of the cell death cascade distal to intramembrane phosphatidylserine translocation.
Effect of the triterpenoid miliacin on the sensitivity of lymphocytes in the thymus and spleen to dexamethasone-induced apoptosis.
N-(3-cyano-4-methyl-2-pyrrylcarbamoylmethyl)iminodiacetic acid derivatives labeled with technetium-99m have been synthesized and evaluated for hepatobiliary activity in rats.
Evaluation of 99mTc-labeled iminodiacetic acid derivatives of substituted 2-aminopyrroles as hepatobiliary imaging agents in rats II.

1-deoxypaclitaxel derivatives can be synthesized from baccatin VI by selective deacylation and C-13 side chain attachment, and some analogues exhibit tubulin polymerization and cytotoxicity activities comparable to paclitaxel.
Synthesis and Biological Evaluation of 1-Deoxypaclitaxel Analogues.

(R)-4-methylcaprolactone can be produced from prochiral 4-methylcyclohexanone via asymmetric chemo-enzymatic Baeyer-Villiger oxidation using (+-)-4-methyloctanoic acid, Candida Antarctica lipase B, and 30% aqueous hydrogen peroxide.

Pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia under air.
Synthesis of pyrazolo[1,5-c]quinazoline derivatives through copper-catalyzed tandem reaction of 5-(2-bromoaryl)-1H-pyrazoles with carbonyl compounds and aqueous ammonia.

2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl is a substituent at the 1 position in the alkoxy pocket of 1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines, and its substitution is explored to enhance the selectivity and potency of PDE5 inhibitors.
1-(2-(2,2,2-trifluoroethoxy)ethyl-1H-pyrazolo[4,3-d]pyrimidines as potent phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) inhibitors.
Ginsenoside Rh7 suppresses the expression of ILF3-AS1, which competitively binds with miR-212, resulting in down-regulation of SMAD1 and suppression of non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) progression.
Ginsenoside Rh7 Suppresses Proliferation, Migration and Invasion of NSCLC Cells Through Targeting ILF3-AS1 Mediated miR-212/SMAD1 Axis.

Pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl isothiocyanate reacts with diamines to form bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas, with yields improved by ultrasound irradiation and reaction times reduced from 7.5 h to 0.75 h compared to conventional methods.
Ultrasound promoted synthesis of bis(substituted pyrazol-4-ylcarbonyl)-substituted thioureas.

Ginsenoside Rh7 can regulate the expression of ILF3-AS1, and by competitively binding with miR-212, it can enhance the miR-212-Smad1 axis, thereby significantly inhibiting the proliferation, migration, and invasion of NSCLC cells.

Ammonium acetate-acetic acid acts as a catalyst for the condensation of ketones with ethyl cyanoacetate to produce ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetate under ultrasound irradiation.
Synthesis of ethyl alkylidene alpha cyanoacetates under ultrasound irradiation.

N-hydroxyindoles can be synthesized in high purity and excellent yield (>90%) via lead-promoted intramolecular reductive cyclization of o-nitrobenzyl ketones and aldehydes under transfer hydrogenation conditions.
A general synthesis of N-hydroxyindoles.

Disodium 1-[2-(carboxylato)pyrrolidin-1-yl]diazen-1-ium-1,2-diolate (PROLI/NO) is a low molecular weight nitric oxide donor that, when incorporated into electrospun polymer fibers, enables nitric oxide release upon immersion in aqueous solutions at physiological pH (7.4, 37°C).
Nitric oxide-releasing electrospun polymer microfibers.
